error\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t// Error implements the error interface.\n25\tfunc (ve LocalValidationError) Error() string {\n26\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n27\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n28\t\t\t\treturn LocalValidationError) String() string {\n37\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n38\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n39\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ValidationError.\n50\tfunc (ve LocalValidationError) ToValidationError() ValidationError {\n51\t\treturn ValidationError{\n52\t\t\tFilePath:   ve.FilePath,\n53\t\t\tMessage:    ve.Message,\n54\t\t\tContextStr: ve.Context,\n55\t\t\tLine:       ve.Line,\n56\t\t\tColumn:     ve.Column,\n57\t\t\tType:       ve.Type,\n58\t\t\tPath:       \"\", // Path is context-specific and populated by caller if needed\n59\t\t}\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t// ValidationResult is defined in result_types.go to consolidate all result types.\n63\t\n64\t// LocalValidationResult provides a validator-specific result type for backward compatibility.\n65\ttype LocalValidationResult struct {\n66\t\tFilePath string                  // Path to the validated file\n67\t\tValid    bool                    // Whether validation passed\n68\t\tErrors   []LocalValidationError  // List of validation errors\n69\t\tWarnings []LocalValidationError  // List of validation warnings\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\t// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\n73\tfunc (vr LocalValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n74\t\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n75\t}\n76\t\n77\t// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\n78\tfunc (vr LocalValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n79\t\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n80\t}\n81\t\n82\t// ErrorSummary and WarningSummary methods for ValidationResult are defined in result_types.go\n83\t\n84\t// Validator provides YAML validation functionality with detailed error reporting.\n85\ttype Validator struct {\n86\t\tstrict bool // Whether to use strict validation\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// NewValidator creates a new YAML validator with default (non-strict) settings.\n90\t//\n91\t// Returns a Validator that checks YAML syntax and structure with lenient validation\n92\t// suitable for development environments. Use NewStrictValidator for production use.\n93\tfunc NewValidator() *Validator {\n94\t\treturn &Validator{\n95\t\t\tstrict: false,\n96\t\t}\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t// NewStrictValidator creates a new YAML validator with strict mode enabled.\n100\t//\n101\t// Returns a Validator that enforces strict YAML validation rules for production\n102\t// environments where errors should not be tolerated and maximum validation coverage is required.\n103\tfunc NewStrictValidator() *Validator {\n104\t\treturn &Validator{\n105\t\t\tstrict: true,\n106\t\t}\n107\t}\n108\t\n109\t// ValidateString validates YAML content from a string.\n110\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult {\n111\t\treturn v.ValidateStringWithPath(yamlContent, \"<string>\")\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\t// ValidateStringWithPath validates YAML content from a string with a file path for error reporting.\n115\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateStringWithPath(yamlContent, filePath string) ValidationResult {\n116\t\tresult := ValidationResult{\n117\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n118\t\t\tValid:    true,\n119\t\t\tErrors:   []ValidationError{},\n120\t\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{},\n121\t\t}\n122\t\n123\t\t// Check for empty content\n124\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(yamlContent) == \"\" {\n125\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n126\t\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n127\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n128\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"YAML content is empty\",\n129\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeEmpty,\n130\t\t\t}\n131\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n132\t\t\treturn result\n133\t\t}\n134\t\n135\t\t// Parse YAML to detect syntax errors\n136\t\tvar node yaml.Node\n137\t\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n138\t\tif err != nil {\n139\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n140\t\t\tve := v.parseYAMLError(err, filePath, yamlContent)\n141\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n142\t\t\treturn result\n143\t\t}\n144\t\n145\t\t// Check for structural issues\n146\t\twarnings := v.checkStructuralIssues(&node, yamlContent)\n147\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, warnings...)\n148\t\n149\t\treturn result\n150\t}\n151\t\n152\t// ValidateFile validates a YAML file at the given path.\n153\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult {\n154\t\tresult := ValidationResult{\n155\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n156\t\t\tValid:    true,\n157\t\t\tErrors:   []ValidationError{},\n158\t\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{},\n159\t\t}\n160\t\n161\t\t// Read file content\n162\t\tcontent, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)\n163\t\tif err != nil {\n164\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n165\t\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n166\t\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n167\t\t\t\t// io.EOF indicates incomplete file or unexpected end of file\n168\t\t\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n169\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n170\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Unexpected end of file - file may be incomplete or truncated\",\n171\t\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeIO,\n172\t\t\t\t\tContext:  \"The file ended before all expected content was read\",\n173\t\t\t\t}\n174\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n175\t\t\t\treturn result\n176\t\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t\t// Check for FileError type with structured information\n179\t\t\tif fileErr, ok := err.(*FileError); ok {\n180\t\t\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n181\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n182\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fileErr.Message,\n183\t\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeFile,\n184\t\t\t\t\tContext:  fileErr.Context(),\n185\t\t\t\t}\n186\t\t\t\tif fileErr.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n187\t\t\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", fileErr.ErrorCode, fileErr.Context())\n188\t\t\t\t}\n189\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n190\t\t\t\treturn result\n191\t\t\t}\n192\t\n193\t\t\t// Check for YAMLError interface\n194\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n195\t\t\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n196\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n197\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  yamlErr.Error(),\n198\t\t\t\t\tType:     yamlErr.YAMLErrorType(),\n199\t\t\t\t\tContext:  yamlErr.Context(),\n200\t\t\t\t}\n201\t\t\t\tif yamlErr.Code() != \"\" {\n202\t\t\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", yamlErr.Code(), yamlErr.Context())\n203\t\t\t\t}\n204\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n205\t\t\t\treturn result\n206\t\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\t\t// Generic error handling\n209\t\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n210\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n211\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %v\", err),\n212\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeIO,\n213\t\t\t}\n214\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n215\t\t\treturn result\n216\t\t}\n217\t\n218\t\t// Validate content\n219\t\treturn v.ValidateStringWithPath(string(content), filePath)\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\t// parseYAMLError converts a yaml.v3 error into a LocalValidationError with line/column info.\n223\tfunc (v *Validator) parseYAMLError(err error, filePath, content string) LocalValidationError {\n224\t\tve := LocalValidationError{\n225\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n226\t\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n227\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeSyntax,\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\t// Standard pattern: sentinel checks → YAMLError interface → specific types → generic fallback\n231\t\n232\t\t// Check for SyntaxError type with structured information\n233\t\tif syntaxErr, ok := err.(*SyntaxError); ok {\n234\t\t\tve.Type = ErrorTypeSyntax\n235\t\t\tve.Message = syntaxErr.Message\n236\t\t\tve.Line = syntaxErr.Line\n237\t\t\tve.Column = syntaxErr.Column\n238\t\t\tve.Context = syntaxErr.Context()\n239\t\t\tif syntaxErr.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n240\t\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", syntaxErr.ErrorCode, syntaxErr.Context())\n241\t\t\t}\n242\t\t\treturn ve\n243\t\t}\n244\t\n245\t\t// Check for StructureError type with structured information\n246\t\tif structErr, ok := err.(*StructureError); ok {\n247\t\t\tve.Type = ErrorTypeStructure\n248\t\t\tve.Message = structErr.Message\n249\t\t\tve.Line = structErr.Line\n250\t\t\tve.Context = structErr.Context()\n251\t\t\tif structErr.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n252\t\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", structErr.ErrorCode, structErr.Context())\n253\t\t\t}\n254\t\t\treturn ve\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\t// Check for YAMLError interface (base interface for all YAML errors)\n258\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n259\t\t\tve.Type = yamlErr.YAMLErrorType()\n260\t\t\tve.Message = yamlErr.Error()\n261\t\t\tve.Context = yamlErr.Context()\n262\t\t\tif yamlErr.Code() != \"\" {\n263\t\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", yamlErr.Code(), yamlErr.Context())\n264\t\t\t}\n265\t\t\treturn ve\n266\t\t}\n267\t\n268\t\t// Check for specific YAML error types using type assertions\n269\t\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n270\t\t\t// This is a YAML type error - provide detailed information\n271\t\t\tve.Type = ErrorTypeStructure\n272\t\t\tve.Message = LocalValidationError provides additional fields for validator-specific error details.\n// This is used internally by the validator and can be converted to the standard ValidationError.\ntype LocalValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tLine     int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn   int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage  string    // Human-readable error message\n\tContext  string    // Contextual information about the error\n\tType     ErrorType // Category of error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve LocalValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: Line %d, Column %d: %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Column, ve.Type, ve.Message)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: Line %d: %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Type, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Type, ve.Message)\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve LocalValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.Type))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d, Column %d\\n\", ve.Line, ve.Column))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ToValidationError converts LocalValidationError to the standard ValidationError.\nfunc (ve LocalValidationError) ToValidationError() ValidationError {\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   ve.FilePath,\n\t\tMessage:    ve.Message,\n\t\tContextStr: ve.Context,\n\t\tLine:       ve.Line,\n\t\tColumn:     ve.Column,\n\t\tType:       ve.Type,\n\t\tPath:       \"\", // Path is context-specific and populated by caller if needed\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidationResult is defined in result_types.go to consolidate all result types.\n\n// LocalValidationResult provides a validator-specific result type for backward compatibility.\ntype LocalValidationResult struct {\n\tFilePath string                  // Path to the validated file\n\tValid    bool                    // Whether validation passed\n\tErrors   []LocalValidationError  // List of validation errors\n\tWarnings []LocalValidationError  // List of validation warnings\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there LocalValidationError{\n\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\tMessage:  \"YAML content is empty\",\n\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeEmpty,\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML to detect syntax errors\n\tvar node yaml.Node\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n\tif err != nil the given path.\nfunc (v *Validator) ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult {\n\tresult := ValidationResult{\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tValid:    true,\n\t\tErrors: read\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ve.ToValidationError())\n\t\t\treturn result\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for FileError type with structured information\n\t\tif fileErr, ok := filePath, content string) LocalValidationError {\n\tve := LocalValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n\t\tType:     ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t}\n\n\t// Standard pattern: ErrorTypeSyntax\n\t\tve.Message = syntaxErr.Message\n\t\tve.Line = syntaxErr.Line\n\t\tve.Column = syntaxErr.Column\n\t\tve.Context = syntaxErr.Context()\n\t\tif syntaxErr.ErrorCode != \"\" interface for all YAML errors)\n\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\tve.Type = yamlErr.YAMLErrorType()\n\t\tve.Message = yamlErr.Error()\n\t\tve.Context = yamlErr.Context()\n\t\tif yamlErr.Code() != \"\" {\n\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"ErrorCode: %s, %s\", yamlErr.Code(), yamlErr.Context())\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ve\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for specific YAML error types using type assertions\n\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n\t\t// This is a YAML type error - provide detailed information\n\t\tve.Type = ErrorTypeStructure\n\t\tve.Message = FormatYAMLErrorMessage(ve.FilePath, typeErr.Errors)\n\t\tif len(typeErr.Errors) > 0 {\n\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"Type errors: %s\", strings.Join(typeErr.Errors, \"; \"))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"line \")\n\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\t\tlineStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n\t\t\tvar line int\n\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(lineStr, \"%d\", &line); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tve.Line = line\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract column if available\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"column \") {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"column \")\n\t\tif strings.ToLower(errMsg)\n\n\t// Check for specific error patterns\n\tswitch {\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"could not find expected\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"did not find expected key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"found character that cannot start any key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"invalid indentation\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"duplicate key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"mapping values are not allowed\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unexpected end\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unacceptable character\"):\n\t\treturn strings.Contains(lower, \"cannot unmarshal\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkStructuralIssues checks for potential structural issues in the YAML.\nfunc (v *Validator) checkStructuralIssues(node *yaml.Node, content string) []ValidationError {\n\tvar warnings []ValidationError\n\n\t// Recursively check for structural string\n277\t\tRawError error\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t// Error implements the error interface.\n281\tfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Error() string {\n282\t\tif ype.Line > 0 {\n283\t\t\tif ype.Column > 0 %s at line %d: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Line, ype.Message)\n287\t\t}\n288\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Message)\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n292\tfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Unwrap() error {\n293\t\treturn ype.RawError\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// NewYAMLParseError creates a new YAMLParseError with the given parameters.\n297\t//\n298\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n299\t// YAMLParseError instances with file path, message, line, column, and optional raw error.\n300\t//\n301\t// Parameters:\n302\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n303\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n304\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n305\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n306\t//   - rawError: Underlying error from the YAML parser (optional, can be nil)\n307\t//\n308\t// Returns a properly initialized YAMLParseError that implements the error interface.\n309\t//\n310\t// Example usage:\n311\t//\n312\t//\terr := NewYAMLParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"unexpected token\", 10, 5, nil)\n313\tfunc NewYAMLParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, rawError error) *YAMLParseError {\n314\t\treturn &YAMLParseError{\n315\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n316\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n317\t\t\tLine:     line,\n318\t\t\tColumn:   column,\n319\t\t\tRawError: rawError,\n320\t\t}\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t// ParseYAML reads and parses a YAML file into a generic map structure.\n324\t// This function provides comprehensive error handling with the following behavior:\n325\t//   - Returns map[string]interface{} and error\n326\t//   - Distinguishes between file I/O errors and YAML syntax errors\n327\t//   - Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n328\t//   - Provides helpful error messages with line numbers for syntax errors\n329\t//   - Uses the file I/O foundation from file.go\n330\t//\n331\t// Returns an empty map for empty or whitespace-only files.\n332\t// Returns YAMLParseError for YAML syntax errors with line/column information.\n333\t// Returns FileError for file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.).\n334\tfunc ParseYAML(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n335\t\t// Use the existing ReadFile function to leverage file I/O error handling\n336\t\tcontent, err := ReadFile(filePath)\n337\t\tif err != nil {\n338\t\t\t// ReadFile already returns FileError with proper context\n339\t\t\treturn nil, err\n340\t\t}\n341\t\n342\t\t// Check for empty file or whitespace-only content\n343\t\tcontentStr := string(content)\n344\t\tif len(contentStr) == 0 || isWhitespace(contentStr) {\n345\t\t\t// Return empty map for empty files - not an error\n346\t\t\treturn make(map[string]interface{}), nil\n347\t\t}\n348\t\n349\t\t// Parse YAML content with detailed error handling\n350\t\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n351\t\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n352\t\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n353\t\n354\t\t\t// Standard pattern: sentinel checks → YAMLError interface → specific types → generic fallback\n355\t\n356\t\t\t// Check for io.EOF sentinel\n357\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {\n358\t\t\t\t// io.EOF indicates incomplete YAML content\n359\t\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n360\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n361\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Unexpected end of YAML content - file may be incomplete\",\n362\t\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n363\t\t\t\t}\n364\t\t\t}\n365\t\n366\t\t\t// Check for SyntaxError type with structured information\n367\t\t\tif syntaxErr, ok := err.(*SyntaxError); ok {\n368\t\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n369\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n370\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  syntaxErr.Message,\n371\t\t\t\t\tLine:     syntaxErr.Line,\n372\t\t\t\t\tColumn:   syntaxErr.Column,\n373\t\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n374\t\t\t\t}\n375\t\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t\t// Check for StructureError type with structured information\n378\t\t\tif structErr, ok := err.(*StructureError); ok {\n379\t\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n380\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n381\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  structErr.Message,\n382\t\t\t\t\tLine:     structErr.Line,\n383\t\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n384\t\t\t\t}\n385\t\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t\t// Check for YAMLError interface (base interface for all YAML errors)\n388\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n389\t\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n390\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n391\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  yamlErr.Error(),\n392\t\t\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n393\t\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n394\t\t\t\t}\n395\t\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\t\t// Type assertion: *yaml.TypeError captures type mismatch errors from yaml.v3\n398\t\t\t// The Errors field contains a slice of error strings detailing each type mismatch\n399\t\t\t// This preserves error information through the type assertion\n400\t\t\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n401\t\t\t\t// Provide detailed type error information\n402\t\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n403\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n404\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  FormatYAMLErrorMessage(filePath, typeErr.Errors),\n405\t\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n406\t\t\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n407\t\t\t\t}\n408\t\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t\t// Generic error fallback with line information\n411\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n412\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n413\t\t\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n414\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n415\t\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n416\t\t\t}\n417\t\t}\n418\t\n419\t\t// Ensure we return a non-nil map even if the YAML contains only null\n420\t\tif data == nil {\n421\t\t\treturn make(map[string]interface{}), nil\n422\t\t}\n423\t\n424\t\treturn data, nil\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\t// isWhitespace checks if a string contains only whitespace characters.\n428\tfunc isWhitespace(s string) bool {\n429\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n430\t\t\tif !isWhitespaceRune(r) {\n431\t\t\t\treturn false\n432\t\t\t}\n433\t\t}\n434\t\treturn true\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\t// isWhitespaceRune checks if a rune is a whitespace character.\n438\tfunc isWhitespaceRune(r rune) bool {\n439\t\treturn r == ' ' || r == '\\t' || r == '\\n' || r == '\\r'\n440\t}\n441\t\n442\t// formatTypeErrorDetails creates a detailed, structured error message from yaml.TypeError.\n443\t// It parses individual error messages and extracts line numbers, type information,\n444\t// and field context to provide actionable debugging information.\n445\t//\n446\t// Example output:\n447\t//   \"YAML type error: 2 type mismatch(es) found\n448\t//    • Line 2: Field 'count' - cannot unmarshal !!str `not an int` into int\n449\t//      Expected: int, Actual: string (YAML tag: !!str)\"\n450\tfunc formatTypeErrorDetails(typeErr *yaml.TypeError) string {\n451\t\tif typeErr == nil || len(typeErr.Errors) == 0 {\n452\t\t\treturn \"YAML type error: no details available\"\n453\t\t}\n454\t\n455\t\t// Header with error count\n456\t\theader := fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML type error: %d type mismatch(es) found\", len(typeErr.Errors))\n457\t\n458\t\t// Process each error to extract structured information\n459\t\tvar detailedErrors []string\n460\t\tfor i, errMsg := range typeErr.Errors {\n461\t\t\tparsed := parseSingleTypeError(errMsg, i+1)\n462\t\t\tdetailedErrors = append(detailedErrors, parsed)\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\t// Combine header with detailed errors\n466\t\treturn header + \"\\n\" + strings.Join(detailedErrors, \"\\n\")\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t// parseSingleTypeError parses a single type error message and formats it with\n470\t// structured information including line number, field context, expected type,\n471\t// and actual type.\n472\t//\n473\t// Input format examples:\n474\t//   - \"line 2: cannot unmarshal !!str `not an int` into int\"\n475\t//   - \"line 3: cannot unmarshal !!map into []string\"\n476\t//\n477\t// Output format:\n478\t//   \"• Error 1: Line 2 - cannot unmarshal string value into int field\n479\t//     Expected: int, Actual: string (YAML tag: !!str), Value: 'not an int'\"\n480\tfunc parseSingleTypeError(errMsg string, errorNum int) string {\n481\t\t// Default structure\n482\t\tresult := struct {\n483\t\t\tline        int\n484\t\t\tyamlTag     string\n485\t\t\tvalue       string\n486\t\t\ttargetType  string\n487\t\t\tcontext     string\n488\t\t}{\n489\t\t\tline:       0,\n490\t\t\tyamlTag:    \"unknown\",\n491\t\t\tvalue:      \"\",\n492\t\t\ttargetType: \"unknown\",\n493\t\t\tcontext:    errMsg,\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\t// Parse line number: \"line 2:\"\n497\t\tif lineMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`line (\\d+):`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(lineMatches) > 1 {\n498\t\t\tif lineNum, err := strconv.Atoi(lineMatches[1]); err == nil {\n499\t\t\t\tresult.line = lineNum\n500\t\t\t}\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\t// Parse YAML tag: \"!!str\", \"!!map\", etc.\n504\t\tif tagMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`!!(\\w+)`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(tagMatches) > 1 {\n505\t\t\tresult.yamlTag = tagMatches[1]\n506\t\t}\n507\t\n508\t\t// Parse quoted value: `not an int`\n509\t\tif valueMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`'([^']+)'`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(valueMatches) > 1 {\n510\t\t\tresult.value = valueMatches[1]\n511\t\t} else if valueMatches := regexp.MustCompile(\"\\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\").FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(valueMatches) > 1 {\n512\t\t\tresult.value = valueMatches[1]\n513\t\t}\n514\t\n515\t\t// Parse target type: \"into int\", \"into []string\", \"into map[string]interface{}\"\n516\t\tif typeMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`into (.+)$`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(typeMatches) > 1 {\n517\t\t\tresult.targetType = strings.TrimSpace(typeMatches[1])\n518\t\t}\n519\t\n520\t\t// Build formatted error message\n521\t\tvar msgBuilder strings.Builder\n522\t\n523\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"• Error %d: \", errorNum))\n524\t\n525\t\tif result.line > 0 {\n526\t\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Line %d - \", result.line))\n527\t\t}\n528\t\n529\t\t// Main error description\n530\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"cannot unmarshal %s value into %s field\",\n531\t\t\tmapYAMLTagToReadable(result.yamlTag), result.targetType))\n532\t\n533\t\t// Add detailed type information\n534\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(\"\\n  \")\n535\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected: %s, Actual: %s (YAML tag: !!%s)\",\n536\t\t\tresult.targetType, mapYAMLTagToReadable(result.yamlTag), result.yamlTag))\n537\t\n538\t\t// Add value preview if available\n539\t\tif result.value != \"\" {\n540\t\t\t// Truncate long values\n541\t\t\tvaluePreview := result.value\n542\t\t\tif len(valuePreview) > 50 {\n543\t\t\t\tvaluePreview = valuePreview[:47] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Value: '%s'\", valuePreview))\n546\t\t}\n547\t\n548\t\treturn msgBuilder.String()\n549\t}\n550\t\n551\t// mapYAMLTagToReadable converts YAML type tags to readable type names.\n552\tfunc mapYAMLTagToReadable(tag string) string {\n553\t\tswitch tag {\n554\t\tcase \"str\":\n555\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n556\t\tcase \"int\":\n557\t\t\treturn \"integer\"\n558\t\tcase \"float\":\n559\t\t\treturn \"float\"\n560\t\tcase \"bool\":\n561\t\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n562\t\tcase \"map\":\n563\t\t\treturn \"map/object\"\n564\t\tcase \"seq\":\n565\t\t\treturn \"array\"\n566\t\tcase \"null\":\n567\t\t\treturn \"null\"\n568\t\tcase \"timestamp\":\n569\t\t\treturn \"timestamp\"\n570\t\tdefault:\n571\t\t\treturn tag\n572\t\t}\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t// extractErrorLine attempts to extract line number from YAML parser errors.\n576\t// The yaml.v3 parser sometimes includes line information in error messages.\n577\tfunc extractErrorLine(err error) int {\n578\t\tif err == nil {\n579\t\t\treturn 0\n580\t\t}\n581\t\n582\t\t// Try to parse line number from common error patterns\n583\t\t// yaml.v3 errors often contain \"line %d\" patterns\n584\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n585\t\n586\t\t// This is a best-effort extraction since yaml.v3 doesn't always provide line info\n587\t\t// The parser typically includes line numbers in syntax errors\n588\t\tvar line int\n589\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, \"yaml: line %d:\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n590\t\t\treturn line\n591\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t// Try alternative patterns\n594\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, \"error at line %d\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n595\t\t\treturn line\n596\t\t}\n597\t\n598\t\treturn 0\n599\t}\n600\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b16057df-92de-40eb-be01-f479bd033fda","uuid":"31eb30c6-c1c7-4ce1-a7a9-c90d77e36a30","timestamp":"2026-07-13T00:50:28.934Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/parser.go","content":"// ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult {\n\tresult := ParseResult{\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t}\n\n\t// Read file content\n\tcontent, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)\n\tif err != nil generic map\n\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.Success = false\n\t\t\t// Type assertion: *yaml.TypeError captures type mismatch errors from yaml.v3\n\t\t\t// The Errors field contains a slice of error strings detailing each type mismatch\n\t\t\t// This preserves error information through the type assertion\n\t\tif typeErr, ok = data\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// MustParseFile reads and parses a YAML file, panicking on any error.\n// This is useful for initialization code where YAML files are critical.\nfunc (p *Parser) MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) {\n\tresult := p.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n\tif !result.Success {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to parse YAML file %s: %v\", filePath, result.Error))\n\t}\n}\n\n// Config returns the configuration for this parser.\nfunc (p *Parser) Config() *ParserConfig {\n\tif p.config == nil {\n\t\tp.config = DefaultParserConfig()\n\t}\n\treturn has a YAML extension (.yaml or .yml).\nfunc IsYAMLFile(filePath string) bool {\n\text := filepath.Ext(filePath)\n\treturn ext == \".yaml\" || ext == \".yml\"\n}\n\n// FindYAMLFiles finds all YAML files in a directory (non-recursive).\nfunc FindYAMLFiles(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n\tentries, err := os.ReadDir(dirPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read directory: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar yamlFiles []string\n\tfor _, entry := range entries {\n\t\tif entry.IsDir() {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfilePath := filepath.Join(dirPath, entry.Name())\n\t\tif IsYAMLFile(filePath) {\n\t\t\tyamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, filePath)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn yamlFiles, nil\n}\n\n// FindYAMLFilesRecursive finds all YAML files in a directory recursively.\nfunc FindYAMLFilesRecursive(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n\tvar yamlFiles []string\n\n\terr := filepath.Walk(dirPath, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !info.IsDir() && IsYAMLFile(path) {\n\t\t\tyamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, path)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to walk directory: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn yamlFiles, nil\n}\n\n// YAMLParseError represents a YAML syntax error with detailed location information.\ntype YAMLParseError struct {\n\tFilePath string\n\tLine     int\n\tColumn   int\n\tMessage  string\n\tRawError error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Error() string {\n\tif ype.Line > 0 {\n\t\tif ype.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Line, ype.Column, ype.Message)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s at line %d: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Line, ype.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ype.RawError\n}\n\n// NewYAMLParseError creates a new YAMLParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// YAMLParseError instances with file path, message, line, column, and optional raw error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - rawError: Underlying error from the YAML parser (optional, can be nil)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized YAMLParseError that implements the error interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewYAMLParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"unexpected token\", 10, 5, nil)\nfunc NewYAMLParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, rawError error) *YAMLParseError {\n\treturn &YAMLParseError{\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLine:     line,\n\t\tColumn:   column,\n\t\tRawError: rawError,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ParseYAML reads and parses a YAML file into a generic map structure.\n// This function provides comprehensive error handling with the following behavior:\n//   - Returns map[string]interface{} and error\n//   - Distinguishes between file I/O errors and YAML syntax errors\n//   - Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n//   - Provides helpful error messages with line numbers for syntax errors\n//   - Uses the file I/O foundation from file.go\n//\n// Returns an empty map for empty or whitespace-only files.\n// Returns YAMLParseError for YAML syntax errors with line/column information.\n// Returns FileError for file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.).\nfunc ParseYAML(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n\t// Use the existing ReadFile function to leverage file I/O error handling\n\tcontent, err := ReadFile(filePath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// ReadFile already returns FileError with proper context\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for empty file or whitespace-only content\n\tcontentStr := string(content)\n\tif len(contentStr) == 0 || isWhitespace(contentStr) {\n\t\t// Return empty map for empty files - not an error\n\t\treturn make(map[string]interface{}), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML content with detailed error handling\n\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n\n\t\t// Standard pattern: sentinel checks → YAMLError interface → specific types → generic fallback\n\n\t\t// Check for io.EOF sentinel\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {\n\t\t\t// io.EOF indicates incomplete YAML content\n\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  \"Unexpected end of YAML content - file may be incomplete\",\n\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for SyntaxError type with structured information\n\t\tif syntaxErr, ok := err.(*SyntaxError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  syntaxErr.Message,\n\t\t\t\tLine:     syntaxErr.Line,\n\t\t\t\tColumn:   syntaxErr.Column,\n\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for StructureError type with structured information\n\t\tif structErr, ok := err.(*StructureError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  structErr.Message,\n\t\t\t\tLine:     structErr.Line,\n\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for YAMLError interface (base interface for all YAML errors)\n\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  yamlErr.Error(),\n\t\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Type assertion: *yaml.TypeError captures type mismatch errors from yaml.v3\n\t\t// The Errors field contains a slice of error strings detailing each type mismatch\n\t\t// This preserves error information through the type assertion\n\t\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n\t\t\t// Provide detailed type error information\n\t\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:  FormatYAMLErrorMessage(filePath, typeErr.Errors),\n\t\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Generic error fallback with line information\n\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure we return a non-nil map even if the YAML contains only null\n\tif data == nil {\n\t\treturn make(map[string]interface{}), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn data, nil\n}\n\n// isWhitespace checks if a string contains only whitespace characters.\nfunc isWhitespace(s string) bool {\n\tfor _, r := range s {\n\t\tif !isWhitespaceRune(r) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// isWhitespaceRune checks if a rune is a whitespace character.\nfunc isWhitespaceRune(r rune) bool {\n\treturn r == ' ' || r == '\\t' || r == '\\n' || r == '\\r'\n}\n\n// formatTypeErrorDetails creates a detailed, structured error message from yaml.TypeError.\n// It parses individual error messages and extracts line numbers, type information,\n// and field context to provide actionable debugging information.\n//\n// Example output:\n//   \"YAML type error: 2 type mismatch(es) found\n//    • Line 2: Field 'count' - cannot unmarshal !!str `not an int` into int\n//      Expected: int, Actual: string (YAML tag: !!str)\"\nfunc formatTypeErrorDetails(typeErr *yaml.TypeError) string {\n\tif typeErr == nil || len(typeErr.Errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"YAML type error: no details available\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Header with error count\n\theader := fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML type error: %d type mismatch(es) found\", len(typeErr.Errors))\n\n\t// Process each error to extract structured information\n\tvar detailedErrors []string\n\tfor i, errMsg := range typeErr.Errors {\n\t\tparsed := parseSingleTypeError(errMsg, i+1)\n\t\tdetailedErrors = append(detailedErrors, parsed)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine header with detailed errors\n\treturn header + \"\\n\" + strings.Join(detailedErrors, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// parseSingleTypeError parses a single type error message and formats it with\n// structured information including line number, field context, expected type,\n// and actual type.\n//\n// Input format examples:\n//   - \"line 2: cannot unmarshal !!str `not an int` into int\"\n//   - \"line 3: cannot unmarshal !!map into []string\"\n//\n// Output format:\n//   \"• Error 1: Line 2 - cannot unmarshal string value into int field\n//     Expected: int, Actual: string (YAML tag: !!str), Value: 'not an int'\"\nfunc parseSingleTypeError(errMsg string, errorNum int) string {\n\t// Default structure\n\tresult := struct {\n\t\tline        int\n\t\tyamlTag     string\n\t\tvalue       string\n\t\ttargetType  string\n\t\tcontext     string\n\t}{\n\t\tline:       0,\n\t\tyamlTag:    \"unknown\",\n\t\tvalue:      \"\",\n\t\ttargetType: \"unknown\",\n\t\tcontext:    errMsg,\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse line number: \"line 2:\"\n\tif lineMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`line (\\d+):`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(lineMatches) > 1 {\n\t\tif lineNum, err := strconv.Atoi(lineMatches[1]); err == nil {\n\t\t\tresult.line = lineNum\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML tag: \"!!str\", \"!!map\", etc.\n\tif tagMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`!!(\\w+)`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(tagMatches) > 1 {\n\t\tresult.yamlTag = tagMatches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse quoted value: `not an int`\n\tif valueMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`'([^']+)'`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(valueMatches) > 1 {\n\t\tresult.value = valueMatches[1]\n\t} else if valueMatches := regexp.MustCompile(\"\\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\").FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(valueMatches) > 1 {\n\t\tresult.value = valueMatches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse target type: \"into int\", \"into []string\", \"into map[string]interface{}\"\n\tif typeMatches := regexp.MustCompile(`into (.+)$`).FindStringSubmatch(errMsg); len(typeMatches) > 1 {\n\t\tresult.targetType = strings.TrimSpace(typeMatches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Build formatted error message\n\tvar msgBuilder strings.Builder\n\n\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"• Error %d: \", errorNum))\n\n\tif result.line > 0 {\n\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Line %d - \", result.line))\n\t}\n\n\t// Main error description\n\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"cannot unmarshal %s value into %s field\",\n\t\tmapYAMLTagToReadable(result.yamlTag), result.targetType))\n\n\t// Add detailed type information\n\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(\"\\n  \")\n\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected: %s, Actual: %s (YAML tag: !!%s)\",\n\t\tresult.targetType, mapYAMLTagToReadable(result.yamlTag), result.yamlTag))\n\n\t// Add value preview if available\n\tif result.value != \"\" {\n\t\t// Truncate long values\n\t\tvaluePreview := result.value\n\t\tif len(valuePreview) > 50 {\n\t\t\tvaluePreview = valuePreview[:47] + \"...\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tmsgBuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Value: '%s'\", valuePreview))\n\t}\n\n\treturn msgBuilder.String()\n}\n\n// mapYAMLTagToReadable converts YAML type tags to readable type names.\nfunc mapYAMLTagToReadable(tag string) string {\n\tswitch tag {\n\tcase \"str\":\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\tcase \"int\":\n\t\treturn \"integer\"\n\tcase \"float\":\n\t\treturn \"float\"\n\tcase \"bool\":\n\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n\tcase \"map\":\n\t\treturn \"map/object\"\n\tcase \"seq\":\n\t\treturn \"array\"\n\tcase \"null\":\n\t\treturn \"null\"\n\tcase \"timestamp\":\n\t\treturn \"timestamp\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn tag\n\t}\n}\n\n// extractErrorLine attempts to extract line number from YAML parser errors.\n// The yaml.v3 parser sometimes includes line information in error messages.\nfunc extractErrorLine(err error) int {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to parse line number from common error patterns\n\t// yaml.v3 errors often contain \"line %d\" patterns\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t// This is a best-effort extraction since yaml.v3 doesn't always provide line info\n\t// The parser typically includes line numbers in syntax errors\n\tvar line int\n\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, \"yaml: line %d:\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n\t\treturn line\n\t}\n\n\t// Try alternative patterns\n\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, \"error at line %d\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n\t\treturn line\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n","numLines":600,"startLine":1,"totalLines":600}}}

%q, found: %q)\", de.Expected, de.Found))\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\treturn sb.String()\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n256\tfunc (de DelimiterError) Code() ErrorCode {\n257\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// YAMLErrorType returns the error type category.\n261\tfunc (de DelimiterError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n262\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n266\tfunc (de DelimiterError) Context() string {\n267\t\treturn de.ContextStr\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// SyntaxWarning represents a non-critical syntax issue.\n271\t//\n272\t// SyntaxWarning captures issues that don't prevent parsing but may\n273\t// indicate problems or potential bugs, such as:\n274\t// - Deprecated syntax usage\n275\t// - Stylistic inconsistencies\n276\t// - Potential ambiguity\n277\ttype SyntaxWarning struct {\n278\t\tFilePath string // Path to the file\n279\t\tLine     int    // Line number (1-indexed)\n280\t\tColumn   int    // Column number (1-indexed)\n281\t\tMessage  string // Warning message\n282\t\tLevel    string // Warning level (\"info\", \"warning\", \"deprecation\")\n283\t}\n284\t\n285\t// Error implements the error interface.\n286\tfunc (sw SyntaxWarning) Error() string {\n287\t\tif sw.Line > 0 {\n288\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"warning at line %d: %s\", sw.Line, sw.Message)\n289\t\t}\n290\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"warning: %s\", sw.Message)\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// SyntaxErrorContext provides contextual lines around an error location.\n294\t//\n295\t// SyntaxErrorContext helps users understand errors by showing the problematic\n296\t// line in context with surrounding lines.\n297\ttype SyntaxErrorContext struct {\n298\t\tErrorLine       int      // The primary error line\n299\t\tStartLine       int      // First line of context\n300\t\tEndLine         int      // Last line of context\n301\t\tLines           []string // The actual line content\n302\t\tPointer         string   // Pointer to error location (e.g., \"    ^\")\n303\t\tIndentSpaces    int      // Number of spaces to indent the pointer\n304\t\tHasError        bool     // Whether this context contains an error\n305\t}\n306\t\n307\t// String returns a formatted error context display.\n308\tfunc (ec SyntaxErrorContext) String() string {\n309\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n310\t\n311\t\tfor i, line := range ec.Lines {\n312\t\t\tlineNum := ec.StartLine + i\n313\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%4d | %s\\n\", lineNum, line))\n314\t\n315\t\t\t// Add pointer for error line\n316\t\t\tif lineNum == ec.ErrorLine && ec.Pointer != \"\" {\n317\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"     | %s%s\\n\", strings.Repeat(\" \", ec.IndentSpaces), ec.Pointer))\n318\t\t\t}\n319\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\treturn sb.String()\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// DefaultSyntaxValidator provides a comprehensive syntax validator implementation.\n325\t//\n326\t// DefaultSyntaxValidator implements the SyntaxValidator interface using the\n327\t// yaml.v3 parser and additional syntax checking logic.\n328\ttype DefaultSyntaxValidator struct {\n329\t\tstrict         bool   // Whether to use strict validation\n330\t\tindentSpaces   int    // Expected number of spaces per indentation level\n331\t\tallowTabs      bool   // Whether to allow tabs in indentation\n332\t\tcontextLines   int    // Number of context lines to provide\n333\t\tignorePatterns []string // Patterns to ignore during validation\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// NewSyntaxValidator creates a new syntax validator with default settings.\n337\t//\n338\t// Returns a SyntaxValidator with sensible defaults for YAML syntax validation:\n339\t// - 2 spaces per indentation level\n340\t// - Tabs not allowed\n341\t// - 2 context lines\n342\tfunc NewSyntaxValidator() *DefaultSyntaxValidator {\n343\t\treturn &DefaultSyntaxValidator{\n344\t\t\tstrict:       false,\n345\t\t\tindentSpaces: 2,\n346\t\t\tallowTabs:    false,\n347\t\t\tcontextLines: 2,\n348\t\t}\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// NewStrictSyntaxValidator creates a strict syntax validator.\n352\t//\n353\t// Returns a SyntaxValidator with strict validation rules enabled for\n354\t// production environments where maximum syntax coverage is required.\n355\tfunc NewStrictSyntaxValidator() *DefaultSyntaxValidator {\n356\t\treturn &DefaultSyntaxValidator{\n357\t\t\tstrict:       true,\n358\t\t\tindentSpaces: 2,\n359\t\t\tallowTabs:    false,\n360\t\t\tcontextLines: 3,\n361\t\t}\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t// ValidateSyntax validates YAML syntax only.\n365\t// The filePath parameter is optional and used for enhanced error reporting; pass empty string if not available.\n366\tfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) ValidateSyntax(yamlContent string, filePath ...string) SyntaxValidationResult {\n367\t\t// Extract optional filePath parameter\n368\t\tfp := \"\"\n369\t\tif len(filePath) > 0 {\n370\t\t\tfp = filePath[0]\n371\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\tresult := SyntaxValidationResult{\n374\t\t\tValid:        true,\n375\t\t\tContextLines: sv.contextLines,\n376\t\t\tFilePath:     fp,\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\t// Count total lines\n380\t\tresult.TotalLines = strings.Count(yamlContent, \"\\n\") + 1\n381\t\n382\t\t// Check for empty content\n383\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(yamlContent) == \"\" {\n384\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n385\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n386\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"YAML content is empty\",\n387\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileEmpty,\n388\t\t\t})\n389\t\t\treturn result\n390\t\t}\n391\t\n392\t\t// Parse YAML to detect syntax errors\n393\t\tvar node yaml.Node\n394\t\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n395\t\tif err != nil {\n396\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n397\t\t\tresult.ParseError = err\n398\t\t\tresult.ErrorLine = sv.extractErrorLine(err.Error())\n399\t\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n400\t\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n401\t\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n402\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Unexpected end of YAML content - content may be incomplete\",\n403\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n404\t\t\t\t})\n405\t\t\t} else {\n406\t\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, sv.convertParseError(err, fp))\n407\t\t\t}\n408\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t// Detect indentation errors\n411\t\tindentErrors := sv.DetectIndentationErrors(yamlContent)\n412\t\tif len(indentErrors) > 0 {\n413\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n414\t\t\tresult.IndentationErrors = append(result.IndentationErrors, indentErrors...)\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t// Detect delimiter errors\n418\t\tdelimiterErrors := sv.DetectDelimiterErrors(yamlContent)\n419\t\tif len(delimiterErrors) > 0 {\n420\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n421\t\t\tresult.DelimiterErrors = append(result.DelimiterErrors, delimiterErrors...)\n422\t\t}\n423\t\n424\t\t// Detect structure errors\n425\t\tstructureErrors := sv.DetectStructureErrors(yamlContent)\n426\t\tif len(structureErrors) > 0 {\n427\t\t\tresult.StructureErrors = append(result.StructureErrors, structureErrors...)\n428\t\t\tif sv.strict {\n429\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n430\t\t\t} else {\n431\t\t\t\t// Add as warnings in non-strict mode\n432\t\t\t\tfor _, se := range structureErrors {\n433\t\t\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, SyntaxWarning{\n434\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage: se.Message,\n435\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:    se.Line,\n436\t\t\t\t\t\tLevel:   \"warning\",\n437\t\t\t\t\t})\n438\t\t\t\t}\n439\t\t\t}\n440\t\t}\n441\t\n442\t\treturn result\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// ValidateSyntaxInFile validates YAML syntax in a file.\n446\tfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) ValidateSyntaxInFile(filePath string) SyntaxValidationResult {\n447\t\tresult := SyntaxValidationResult{\n448\t\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n449\t\t\tValid:        true,\n450\t\t\tContextLines: sv.contextLines,\n451\t\t}\n452\t\n453\t\t// Read file content\n454\t\tcontent, err := ReadFile(filePath)\n455\t\tif err != nil {\n456\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n457\t\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n458\t\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n459\t\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n460\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Unexpected end of file - file may be incomplete or truncated\",\n461\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n462\t\t\t\t})\n463\t\t\t\treturn result\n464\t\t\t}\n465\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n466\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %v\", err),\n467\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n468\t\t\t})\n469\t\t\treturn result\n470\t\t}\n471\t\n472\t\t// Validate content\n473\t\tcontentResult := sv.ValidateSyntax(string(content), filePath)\n474\t\treturn contentResult\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// DetectIndentationErrors detects indentation inconsistencies in YAML.\n478\tfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) DetectIndentationErrors(yamlContent string) []IndentationError {\n479\t\terrors := make([]IndentationError, 0)\n480\t\tlines := strings.Split(yamlContent, \"\\n\")\n481\t\n482\t\tfor lineNum, line := range lines {\n483\t\t\t// Skip empty lines and comment lines\n484\t\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, \" \\t\")\n485\t\t\tif trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\") {\n486\t\t\t\tcontinue\n487\t\t\t}\n488\t\n489\t\t\t// Count leading whitespace\n490\t\t\tleadingSpaces := 0\n491\t\t\tleadingTabs := 0\n492\t\t\tfor _, ch := range line {\n493\t\t\t\tif ch == ' ' {\n494\t\t\t\t\tleadingSpaces++\n495\t\t\t\t} else if ch == '\\t' {\n496\t\t\t\t\tleadingTabs++\n497\t\t\t\t} else {\n498\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n499\t\t\t\t}\n500\t\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\t\t// Check for mixed tabs and spaces\n503\t\t\tif leadingSpaces > 0 && leadingTabs > 0 {\n504\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n505\t\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n506\t\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n507\t\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\",\n508\t\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"mixed\",\n509\t\t\t\t\tContextStr:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Line has %d spaces and %d tabs\", leadingSpaces, leadingTabs),\n510\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: \"Use only spaces or only tabs for indentation\",\n511\t\t\t\t})\n512\t\t\t\tcontinue\n513\t\t\t}\n514\t\n515\t\t\t// Check if tabs are used when not allowed\n516\t\t\tif leadingTabs > 0 && !sv.allowTabs {\n517\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n518\t\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n519\t\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n520\t\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Tabs not allowed in indentation\",\n521\t\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"tab\",\n522\t\t\t\t\tActual:       leadingTabs * 4, // Assume tab = 4 spaces for display\n523\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: \"Replace tabs with spaces\",\n524\t\t\t\t})\n525\t\t\t\tcontinue\n526\t\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t\t// Check if indentation is a multiple of indentSpaces\n529\t\t\tif leadingSpaces > 0 && sv.indentSpaces > 0 {\n530\t\t\t\tif leadingSpaces%sv.indentSpaces != 0 {\n531\t\t\t\t\texpectedLevel := ((leadingSpaces / sv.indentSpaces) + 1) * sv.indentSpaces\n532\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n533\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n534\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n535\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Indentation not a multiple of expected level\",\n536\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:     expectedLevel,\n537\t\t\t\t\t\tActual:       leadingSpaces,\n538\t\t\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"space\",\n539\t\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: fmt.Sprintf(\"Use %d spaces per indentation level\", sv.indentSpaces),\n540\t\t\t\t\t})\n541\t\t\t\t}\n542\t\t\t}\n543\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\treturn errors\n546\t}\n547\t\n548\t// DetectDelimiterErrors detects delimiter issues in YAML.\n549\tfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) DetectDelimiterErrors(yamlContent string) []DelimiterError {\n550\t\terrors := make([]DelimiterError, 0)\n551\t\tlines := strings.Split(yamlContent, \"\\n\")\n552\t\n553\t\t// Track delimiter balance\n554\t\tparenStack := make([]rune, 0)\n555\t\tbracketStack := make([]rune, 0)\n556\t\tbraceStack := make([]rune, 0)\n557\t\n558\t\t// Track multi-line string blocks\n559\t\tinMultiLineBlock := false\n560\t\tmultiLineBlockIndent := 0\n561\t\n562\t\n563\t\tfor lineNum, line := range lines {\n564\t\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, \" \\t\")\n565\t\t\tleadingSpaces := len(line) - len(trimmed)\n566\t\n567\t\t\t// Check if we're inside a multi-line block\n568\t\t\tif inMultiLineBlock {\n569\t\t\t\t// Multi-line blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n570\t\t\t\t// that's not empty or a comment\n571\t\t\t\tif leadingSpaces > multiLineBlockIndent ||\n572\t\t\t\t   (leadingSpaces == multiLineBlockIndent && (trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\"))) {\n573\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip lines inside the multi-line block\n574\t\t\t\t} else {\n575\t\t\t\t\t// Exit multi-line block when indentation decreases\n576\t\t\t\t\tinMultiLineBlock = false\n577\t\t\t\t\tmultiLineBlockIndent = 0\n578\t\t\t\t}\n579\t\t\t}\n580\t\n581\t\t\t// Skip empty lines and comments (but don't skip them inside multi-line blocks - handled above)\n582\t\t\tif trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\") {\n583\t\t\t\tcontinue\n584\t\t\t}\n585\t\n586\t\t\t// Handle multi-line block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n587\t\t\t// These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n588\t\t\tif strings.Contains(trimmed, \": |\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \": >\") ||\n589\t\t\t   strings.Contains(trimmed, \":|-\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \":>-\") ||\n590\t\t\t   strings.Contains(trimmed, \":|+\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \":>+\") {\n591\t\t\t\tinMultiLineBlock = true\n592\t\t\t\tmultiLineBlockIndent = leadingSpaces // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n593\t\t\t}\n594\t\n595\t\t\t// Check for missing colons in mapping lines\n596\t\t\t// A mapping line should have a colon unless it's a sequence item or flow style\n597\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"- \") &&\n598\t\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"---\") &&\n599\t\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"...\") &&\n600\t\t\t   !strings.Contains(trimmed, \"{\") &&\n601\t\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"&\") &&\n602\t\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"*\") &&\n603\t\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"!!\") {\n604\t\n605\t\t\t\t// Check if this looks like a mapping line (starts with alphanumeric or quote)\n606\t\t\t\tif len(trimmed) > 0 {\n607\t\t\t\t\tfirstChar := rune(trimmed[0])\n608\t\t\t\t\tisMappingCandidate := unicode.IsLetter(firstChar) || unicode.IsDigit(firstChar) ||\n609\t\t\t\t\t                      firstChar == '\\'' || firstChar == '\"' || firstChar == '_'\n610\t\n611\t\t\t\t\t// Additional check: if the line has less indentation than previous line,\n612\t\t\t\t\t// it's more likely to be a key at a higher level\n613\t\t\t\t\tif isMappingCandidate && !strings.Contains(trimmed, \":\") {\n614\t\t\t\t\t\t// Extract the first word to see if it looks like a key\n615\t\t\t\t\t\twords := strings.Fields(trimmed)\n616\t\t\t\t\t\tif len(words) > 0 {\n617\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfirstWord := words[0]\n618\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Check if first word looks like a YAML key (alphanumeric with possible underscores)\n619\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlooksLikeKey := true\n620\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, ch := range firstWord {\n621\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif !unicode.IsLetter(ch) && !unicode.IsDigit(ch) && ch != '_' && ch != '-' && ch != '.' {\n622\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlooksLikeKey = false\n623\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n624\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n625\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n626\t\n627\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif looksLikeKey {\n628\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n629\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n630\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        leadingSpaces + 1,\n631\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Missing colon in mapping key %q\", firstWord),\n632\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \":\",\n633\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"no colon\",\n634\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \":\",\n635\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Add colon after key %q\", firstWord),\n636\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"missing_colon\",\n637\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n638\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n639\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n640\t\t\t\t\t}\n641\t\t\t\t}\n642\t\t\t}\n643\t\n644\t\t\tinString := false\n645\t\t\tstringChar := rune(0)\n646\t\n647\t\t\tfor colNum, ch := range line {\n648\t\t\t\t// Handle string context\n649\t\t\t\tif ch == '\\'' || ch == '\"' {\n650\t\t\t\t\tif !inString {\n651\t\t\t\t\t\tinString = true\n652\t\t\t\t\t\tstringChar = ch\n653\t\t\t\t\t} else if ch == stringChar {\n654\t\t\t\t\t\t// Check for escape\n655\t\t\t\t\t\tif colNum == 0 || line[colNum-1] != '\\\\' {\n656\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinString = false\n657\t\t\t\t\t\t\tstringChar = 0\n658\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n659\t\t\t\t\t}\n660\t\t\t\t}\n661\t\n662\t\t\t\t// Skip delimiter checking inside strings\n663\t\t\t\tif inString {\n664\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n665\t\t\t\t}\n666\t\n667\t\t\t\tswitch ch {\n668\t\t\t\tcase '(':\n669\t\t\t\t\tparenStack = append(parenStack, ch)\n670\t\t\t\tcase ')':\n671\t\t\t\t\tif len(parenStack) == 0 || parenStack[len(parenStack)-1] != '(' {\n672\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n673\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n674\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n675\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing parenthesis\",\n676\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \")\",\n677\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \")\",\n678\t\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"(\",\n679\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_paren\",\n680\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n681\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n682\t\t\t\t\t\tparenStack = parenStack[:len(parenStack)-1]\n683\t\t\t\t\t}\n684\t\t\t\tcase '[':\n685\t\t\t\t\tbracketStack = append(bracketStack, ch)\n686\t\t\t\tcase ']':\n687\t\t\t\t\tif len(bracketStack) == 0 || bracketStack[len(bracketStack)-1] != '[' {\n688\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n689\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n690\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n691\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing bracket\",\n692\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \"]\",\n693\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"]\",\n694\t\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"[\",\n695\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_bracket\",\n696\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n697\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n698\t\t\t\t\t\tbracketStack = bracketStack[:len(bracketStack)-1]\n699\t\t\t\t\t}\n700\t\t\t\tcase '{':\n701\t\t\t\t\tbraceStack = append(braceStack, ch)\n702\t\t\t\tcase '}':\n703\t\t\t\t\tif len(braceStack) == 0 || braceStack[len(braceStack)-1] != '{' {\n704\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n705\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n706\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n707\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing brace\",\n708\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \"}\",\n709\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"}\",\n710\t\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"{\",\n711\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_brace\",\n712\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n713\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n714\t\t\t\t\t\tbraceStack = braceStack[:len(braceStack)-1]\n715\t\t\t\t\t}\n716\t\t\t\tcase ':':\n717\t\t\t\t\t// Check for invalid colon spacing (not followed by space or end of line)\n718\t\t\t\t\tif colNum+1 < len(line) && line[colNum+1] != ' ' && line[colNum+1] != '\\t' && line[colNum+1] != '\\n' && line[colNum+1] != '\\r' {\n719\t\t\t\t\t\t// This might be valid in flow collections, but could be an error in block mappings\n720\t\t\t\t\t\t// Only warn in strict mode for non-flow contexts\n721\t\t\t\t\t\tif sv.strict && {\n738\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n739\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n740\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        len(line) + 1,\n741\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unclosed string at end of with matching quote\",\n746\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unclosed_string\",\n747\t\t\t\t})\n748\t\t\t}\n749\t\t}\n750\t\n751\t\t// Check for unmatched delimiters at end of file\n752\t\tfor _, paren := file\",\n756\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: string(paren),\n757\t\t\t\tExpected:      \")\",\n758\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_paren\",\n759\t\t\t})\n760\t\t}\n761\t\n762\t\tfor _, bracket := range \"}\",\n778\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_brace\",\n779\t\t\t})\n780\t\t}\n781\t\n782\t\treturn errors\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\t// DetectStructureErrors detects structural issues in YAML.\n786\tfunc
