Package yamlutil provides YAML syntax validation interfaces and implementations.\n//\n// The syntax validator provides detailed detection and reporting of YAML syntax errors,\n// including indentation errors, delimiter errors, and structure errors.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"unicode\"\n\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\n// SyntaxValidator defines the interface for YAML syntax error detection.\n//\n// SyntaxValidator implementations detect and report syntax-level errors in YAML\n// content, providing detailed location information and error categorization.\n// This interface extends beyond basic parsing to provide comprehensive syntax\n// error detection before full parsing attempts.\ntype SyntaxValidator interface {\n\t// ValidateSyntax validates YAML syntax only, without semantic validation.\n\t// Returns a SyntaxValidationResult with detailed syntax error information.\n\tValidateSyntax(yamlContent string) SyntaxValidationResult\n\n\t// ValidateSyntaxInFile validates YAML syntax in a file.\n\t// Returns a SyntaxValidationResult with detailed syntax error information.\n\tValidateSyntaxInFile(filePath string) SyntaxValidationResult\n\n\t// DetectIndentationErrors detects indentation inconsistencies in YAML.\n\t// Returns a list of indentation errors with line and column information.\n\tDetectIndentationErrors(yamlContent string) []IndentationError\n\n\t// DetectDelimiterErrors detects delimiter issues (braces, brackets, colons).\n\t// Returns a list of delimiter errors with location information.\n\tDetectDelimiterErrors(yamlContent string) []DelimiterError\n\n\t// DetectStructureErrors detects structural issues before full parsing.\n\t// Returns a list of structure errors with context.\n\tDetectStructureErrors(yamlContent string) []StructureError\n\n\t// GetErrorContext provides contextual information around an error location.\n\t// Returns lines before and after the error for better error reporting.\n\tGetErrorContext(content string, line int, contextLines int) SyntaxErrorContext\n}\n\n// SyntaxValidationResult represents the result of syntax validation.\n//\n// SyntaxValidationResult provides comprehensive information about syntax\n// validation operations, including errors found, warnings, and contextual\n// information to help diagnose and fix syntax issues.\ntype SyntaxValidationResult struct {\n\tFilePath        string                   // Path to the file (if applicable)\n\tValid           bool                     // Whether syntax is valid\n\tSyntaxErrors    []SyntaxError            // List of syntax errors detected\n\tIndentationErrors []IndentationError    // List of indentation errors\n\tDelimiterErrors []DelimiterError        // List of delimiter errors\n\tStructureErrors []StructureError        // List of structure errors\n\tWarnings        []SyntaxWarning         // List of warnings (non-blocking issues)\n\tParseError      error                   // Underlying parse error if any\n\tContextLines    int                     // Number of context lines to provide\n\tTotalLines      int                     // Total lines in the validated content\n\tErrorLine       int                     // Primary error line (if any)\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if any syntax errors were detected.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.SyntaxErrors) > 0 ||\n\t\tlen(svr.IndentationErrors) > 0 ||\n\t\tlen(svr.DelimiterErrors) > 0 ||\n\t\tlen(svr.StructureErrors) > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if any warnings were generated.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorCount returns the total number of errors detected.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) ErrorCount() int {\n\treturn len(svr.SyntaxErrors) +\n\t\tlen(svr.IndentationErrors) +\n\t\tlen(svr.DelimiterErrors) +\n\t\tlen(svr.StructureErrors)\n}\n\n// WarningCount returns the total number of warnings.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) WarningCount() int {\n\treturn len(svr.Warnings)\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all errors.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif svr.Valid {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Syntax validation passed\")\n\t\tif svr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" with %d warning(s)\", svr.WarningCount()))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Syntax validation failed with %d error(s)\", svr.ErrorCount()))\n\n\t\tif svr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" and %d warning(s)\", svr.WarningCount()))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Error returns a formatted error message if validation failed.\nfunc (svr SyntaxValidationResult) Error() string {\n\tif svr.Valid {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Syntax validation failed for %s:\\n\", svr.FilePath))\n\n\t// Add syntax errors\n\tfor _, err := range svr.SyntaxErrors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\\n\", err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add indentation errors\n\tfor _, err := range svr.IndentationErrors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\\n\", err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add delimiter errors\n\tfor _, err := range svr.DelimiterErrors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\\n\", err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add structure errors\n\tfor _, err := range svr.StructureErrors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\\n\", err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IndentationError represents an indentation error in YAML.\n//\n// IndentationError captures issues with YAML indentation, including:\n// - Mixed tabs and spaces\n// - Inconsistent indentation levels\n// - Invalid indentation for nesting level\n// - Incorrect indentation for mapping/sequence items\ntype IndentationError struct {\n\tFilePath     string   // Path to the file\n\tLine         int      // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn       int      // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage      string   // Description of the indentation error\n\tExpected     int      // Expected indentation level (in spaces)\n\tActual       int      // Actual indentation level (in spaces)\n\tIndentType  string   // Type of indentation used (\"space\", \"tab\", \"mixed\")\n\tContextStr   string   // Contextual information (renamed to avoid method conflict)\n\tSuggestedFix string  // Suggested fix for the indentation issue\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ie IndentationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif ie.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"indentation error at line %d\", ie.Line))\n\t\tif ie.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ie.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"indentation error\")\n\t}\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ie.Message))\n\n\tif ie.Expected > 0 || ie.Actual > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %d spaces, actual: %d spaces)\", ie.Expected, ie.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif ie.IndentType != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [type: %s]\", ie.IndentType))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (ie IndentationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType returns the error type category.\nfunc (ie IndentationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context returns additional context about the error.\nfunc (ie IndentationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ie.ContextStr\n}\n\n// DelimiterError represents a delimiter error in YAML.\n//\n// DelimiterError captures issues with YAML delimiters, including:\n// - Unmatched braces, brackets, or quotes\n// - Missing colons in mappings\n// - Invalid use of flow vs block style delimiters\n// - Improper delimiter escaping\ntype DelimiterError struct {\n\tFilePath       string // Path to the file\n\tLine           int    // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn         int    // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage        string // Description of the delimiter error\n\tDelimiterType  string // Type of delimiter (\"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \":\", \"'\", \"\\\"\", \"|\", \">\")\n\tExpected       string // Expected delimiter (if mismatch)\n\tFound          string // Actual delimiter found\n\tContextStr     string // Contextual information (renamed to avoid method conflict)\n\tSuggestedFix   string // Suggested fix for the delimiter issue\n\tErrorCategory  string // Classification of error (\"missing_colon\", \"unmatched_bracket\", \"unmatched_brace\", \"unclosed_string\", \"invalid_spacing\")\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (de DelimiterError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif de.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"delimiter error at line %d\", de.Line))\n\t\tif de.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", de.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"delimiter error\")\n\t}\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", de.Message))\n\n\tif de.DelimiterType != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [delimiter: %s]\", de.DelimiterType))\n\t}\n\n\tif de.ErrorCategory != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [category: %s]\", de.ErrorCategory))\n\t}\n\n\tif de.Expected != \"\" || de.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %q, found: %q)\", de.Expected, de.Found))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (de DelimiterError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType returns the error type category.\nfunc (de DelimiterError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context returns additional context about the error.\nfunc (de DelimiterError) Context() string {\n\treturn de.ContextStr\n}\n\n// SyntaxWarning represents a non-critical syntax issue.\n//\n// SyntaxWarning captures issues that don't prevent parsing but may\n// indicate problems or potential bugs, such as:\n// - Deprecated syntax usage\n// - Stylistic inconsistencies\n// - Potential ambiguity\ntype SyntaxWarning struct {\n\tFilePath string // Path to the file\n\tLine     int    // Line number (1-indexed)\n\tColumn   int    // Column number (1-indexed)\n\tMessage  string // Warning message\n\tLevel    string // Warning level (\"info\", \"warning\", \"deprecation\")\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (sw SyntaxWarning) Error() string {\n\tif sw.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"warning at line %d: %s\", sw.Line, sw.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"warning: %s\", sw.Message)\n}\n\n// SyntaxErrorContext provides contextual lines around an error location.\n//\n// SyntaxErrorContext helps users understand errors by showing the problematic\n// line in context with surrounding lines.\ntype SyntaxErrorContext struct {\n\tErrorLine       int      // The primary error line\n\tStartLine       int      // First line of context\n\tEndLine         int      // Last line of context\n\tLines           []string // The actual line content\n\tPointer         string   // Pointer to error location (e.g., \"    ^\")\n\tIndentSpaces    int      // Number of spaces to indent the pointer\n\tHasError        bool     // Whether this context contains an error\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error context display.\nfunc (ec SyntaxErrorContext) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tfor i, line := range ec.Lines {\n\t\tlineNum := ec.StartLine + i\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%4d | %s\\n\", lineNum, line))\n\n\t\t// Add pointer for error line\n\t\tif lineNum == ec.ErrorLine && ec.Pointer != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"     | %s%s\\n\", strings.Repeat(\" \", ec.IndentSpaces), ec.Pointer))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// DefaultSyntaxValidator provides a comprehensive syntax validator implementation.\n//\n// DefaultSyntaxValidator implements the SyntaxValidator interface using the\n// yaml.v3 parser and additional syntax checking logic.\ntype DefaultSyntaxValidator struct {\n\tstrict         bool   // Whether to use strict validation\n\tindentSpaces   int    // Expected number of spaces per indentation level\n\tallowTabs      bool   // Whether to allow tabs in indentation\n\tcontextLines   int    // Number of context lines to provide\n\tignorePatterns []string // Patterns to ignore during validation\n}\n\n// NewSyntaxValidator creates a new syntax validator with default settings.\n//\n// Returns a SyntaxValidator with sensible defaults for YAML syntax validation:\n// - 2 spaces per indentation level\n// - Tabs not allowed\n// - 2 context lines\nfunc NewSyntaxValidator() *DefaultSyntaxValidator {\n\treturn &DefaultSyntaxValidator{\n\t\tstrict:       false,\n\t\tindentSpaces: 2,\n\t\tallowTabs:    false,\n\t\tcontextLines: 2,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStrictSyntaxValidator creates a strict syntax validator.\n//\n// Returns a SyntaxValidator with strict validation rules enabled for\n// production environments where maximum syntax coverage is required.\nfunc NewStrictSyntaxValidator() *DefaultSyntaxValidator {\n\treturn &DefaultSyntaxValidator{\n\t\tstrict:       true,\n\t\tindentSpaces: 2,\n\t\tallowTabs:    false,\n\t\tcontextLines: 3,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateSyntax validates YAML syntax only.\n// The filePath parameter is optional and used for enhanced error reporting; pass empty string if not available.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) ValidateSyntax(yamlContent string, filePath ...string) SyntaxValidationResult {\n\t// Extract optional filePath parameter\n\tfp := \"\"\n\tif len(filePath) > 0 {\n\t\tfp = filePath[0]\n\t}\n\n\tresult := SyntaxValidationResult{\n\t\tValid:        true,\n\t\tContextLines: sv.contextLines,\n\t\tFilePath:     fp,\n\t}\n\n\t// Count total lines\n\tresult.TotalLines = strings.Count(yamlContent, \"\\n\") + 1\n\n\t// Check for empty content\n\tif strings.TrimSpace(yamlContent) == \"\" {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n\t\t\tMessage:   \"YAML content is empty\",\n\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileEmpty,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML to detect syntax errors\n\tvar node yaml.Node\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tresult.ParseError = err\n\t\tresult.ErrorLine = sv.extractErrorLine(err.Error())\n\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Unexpected end of YAML content - content may be incomplete\",\n\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, sv.convertParseError(err, fp))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Detect indentation errors\n\tindentErrors := sv.DetectIndentationErrors(yamlContent)\n\tif len(indentErrors) > 0 {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tresult.IndentationErrors = append(result.IndentationErrors, indentErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Detect delimiter errors\n\tdelimiterErrors := sv.DetectDelimiterErrors(yamlContent)\n\tif len(delimiterErrors) > 0 {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tresult.DelimiterErrors = append(result.DelimiterErrors, delimiterErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Detect structure errors\n\tstructureErrors := sv.DetectStructureErrors(yamlContent)\n\tif len(structureErrors) > 0 {\n\t\tresult.StructureErrors = append(result.StructureErrors, structureErrors...)\n\t\tif sv.strict {\n\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Add as warnings in non-strict mode\n\t\t\tfor _, se := range structureErrors {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, SyntaxWarning{\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: se.Message,\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:    se.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\tLevel:   \"warning\",\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ValidateSyntaxInFile validates YAML syntax in a file.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) ValidateSyntaxInFile(filePath string) SyntaxValidationResult {\n\tresult := SyntaxValidationResult{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tValid:        true,\n\t\tContextLines: sv.contextLines,\n\t}\n\n\t// Read file content\n\tcontent, err := ReadFile(filePath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\t// Check for specific error types using type assertions\n\t\tif err == io.EOF {\n\t\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Unexpected end of file - file may be incomplete or truncated\",\n\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\treturn result\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult.SyntaxErrors = append(result.SyntaxErrors, SyntaxError{\n\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %v\", err),\n\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeFileIOError,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content\n\tcontentResult := sv.ValidateSyntax(string(content), filePath)\n\treturn contentResult\n}\n\n// DetectIndentationErrors detects indentation inconsistencies in YAML.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) DetectIndentationErrors(yamlContent string) []IndentationError {\n\terrors := make([]IndentationError, 0)\n\tlines := strings.Split(yamlContent, \"\\n\")\n\n\tfor lineNum, line := range lines {\n\t\t// Skip empty lines and comment lines\n\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, \" \\t\")\n\t\tif trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\") {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Count leading whitespace\n\t\tleadingSpaces := 0\n\t\tleadingTabs := 0\n\t\tfor _, ch := range line {\n\t\t\tif ch == ' ' {\n\t\t\t\tleadingSpaces++\n\t\t\t} else if ch == '\\t' {\n\t\t\t\tleadingTabs++\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for mixed tabs and spaces\n\t\tif leadingSpaces > 0 && leadingTabs > 0 {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\",\n\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"mixed\",\n\t\t\t\tContextStr:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Line has %d spaces and %d tabs\", leadingSpaces, leadingTabs),\n\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: \"Use only spaces or only tabs for indentation\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if tabs are used when not allowed\n\t\tif leadingTabs > 0 && !sv.allowTabs {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Tabs not allowed in indentation\",\n\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"tab\",\n\t\t\t\tActual:       leadingTabs * 4, // Assume tab = 4 spaces for display\n\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: \"Replace tabs with spaces\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if indentation is a multiple of indentSpaces\n\t\tif leadingSpaces > 0 && sv.indentSpaces > 0 {\n\t\t\tif leadingSpaces%sv.indentSpaces != 0 {\n\t\t\t\texpectedLevel := ((leadingSpaces / sv.indentSpaces) + 1) * sv.indentSpaces\n\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, IndentationError{\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:         lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\tColumn:       1,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:      \"Indentation not a multiple of expected level\",\n\t\t\t\t\tExpected:     expectedLevel,\n\t\t\t\t\tActual:       leadingSpaces,\n\t\t\t\t\tIndentType:   \"space\",\n\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix: fmt.Sprintf(\"Use %d spaces per indentation level\", sv.indentSpaces),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// DetectDelimiterErrors detects delimiter issues in YAML.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) DetectDelimiterErrors(yamlContent string) []DelimiterError {\n\terrors := make([]DelimiterError, 0)\n\tlines := strings.Split(yamlContent, \"\\n\")\n\n\t// Track delimiter balance\n\tparenStack := make([]rune, 0)\n\tbracketStack := make([]rune, 0)\n\tbraceStack := make([]rune, 0)\n\n\t// Track multi-line string blocks\n\tinMultiLineBlock := false\n\tmultiLineBlockIndent := 0\n\n\n\tfor lineNum, line := range lines {\n\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, \" \\t\")\n\t\tleadingSpaces := len(line) - len(trimmed)\n\n\t\t// Check if we're inside a multi-line block\n\t\tif inMultiLineBlock {\n\t\t\t// Multi-line blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n\t\t\t// that's not empty or a comment\n\t\t\tif leadingSpaces > multiLineBlockIndent ||\n\t\t\t   (leadingSpaces == multiLineBlockIndent && (trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\"))) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip lines inside the multi-line block\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Exit multi-line block when indentation decreases\n\t\t\t\tinMultiLineBlock = false\n\t\t\t\tmultiLineBlockIndent = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip empty lines and comments (but don't skip them inside multi-line blocks - handled above)\n\t\tif trimmed == \"\" || strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"#\") {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Handle multi-line block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n\t\t// These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n\t\tif strings.Contains(trimmed, \": |\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \": >\") ||\n\t\t   strings.Contains(trimmed, \":|-\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \":>-\") ||\n\t\t   strings.Contains(trimmed, \":|+\") || strings.Contains(trimmed, \":>+\") {\n\t\t\tinMultiLineBlock = true\n\t\t\tmultiLineBlockIndent = leadingSpaces // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing colons in mapping lines\n\t\t// A mapping line should have a colon unless it's a sequence item or flow style\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"- \") &&\n\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"---\") &&\n\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"...\") &&\n\t\t   !strings.Contains(trimmed, \"{\") &&\n\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"&\") &&\n\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"*\") &&\n\t\t   !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"!!\") {\n\n\t\t\t// Check if this looks like a mapping line (starts with alphanumeric or quote)\n\t\t\tif len(trimmed) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tfirstChar := rune(trimmed[0])\n\t\t\t\tisMappingCandidate := unicode.IsLetter(firstChar) || unicode.IsDigit(firstChar) ||\n\t\t\t\t                      firstChar == '\\'' || firstChar == '\"' || firstChar == '_'\n\n\t\t\t\t// Additional check: if the line has less indentation than previous line,\n\t\t\t\t// it's more likely to be a key at a higher level\n\t\t\t\tif isMappingCandidate && !strings.Contains(trimmed, \":\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Extract the first word to see if it looks like a key\n\t\t\t\t\twords := strings.Fields(trimmed)\n\t\t\t\t\tif len(words) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfirstWord := words[0]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Check if first word looks like a YAML key (alphanumeric with possible underscores)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlooksLikeKey := true\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, ch := range firstWord {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif !unicode.IsLetter(ch) && !unicode.IsDigit(ch) && ch != '_' && ch != '-' && ch != '.' {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlooksLikeKey = false\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif looksLikeKey {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        leadingSpaces + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Missing colon in mapping key %q\", firstWord),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \":\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"no colon\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \":\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Add colon after key %q\", firstWord),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"missing_colon\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinString := false\n\t\tstringChar := rune(0)\n\n\t\tfor colNum, ch := range line {\n\t\t\t// Handle string context\n\t\t\tif ch == '\\'' || ch == '\"' {\n\t\t\t\tif !inString {\n\t\t\t\t\tinString = true\n\t\t\t\t\tstringChar = ch\n\t\t\t\t} else if ch == stringChar {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Check for escape\n\t\t\t\t\tif colNum == 0 || line[colNum-1] != '\\\\' {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tinString = false\n\t\t\t\t\t\tstringChar = 0\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Skip delimiter checking inside strings\n\t\t\tif inString {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tswitch ch {\n\t\t\tcase '(':\n\t\t\t\tparenStack = append(parenStack, ch)\n\t\t\tcase ')':\n\t\t\t\tif len(parenStack) == 0 || parenStack[len(parenStack)-1] != '(' {\n\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing parenthesis\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \")\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \")\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"(\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_paren\",\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tparenStack = parenStack[:len(parenStack)-1]\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase '[':\n\t\t\t\tbracketStack = append(bracketStack, ch)\n\t\t\tcase ']':\n\t\t\t\tif len(bracketStack) == 0 || bracketStack[len(bracketStack)-1] != '[' {\n\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing bracket\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \"]\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"]\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"[\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_bracket\",\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tbracketStack = bracketStack[:len(bracketStack)-1]\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase '{':\n\t\t\t\tbraceStack = append(braceStack, ch)\n\t\t\tcase '}':\n\t\t\t\tif len(braceStack) == 0 || braceStack[len(braceStack)-1] != '{' {\n\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unmatched closing brace\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \"}\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         \"}\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tExpected:      \"{\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_brace\",\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tbraceStack = braceStack[:len(braceStack)-1]\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase ':':\n\t\t\t\t// Check for invalid colon spacing (not followed by space or end of line)\n\t\t\t\tif colNum+1 < len(line) && line[colNum+1] != ' ' && line[colNum+1] != '\\t' && line[colNum+1] != '\\n' && line[colNum+1] != '\\r' {\n\t\t\t\t\t// This might be valid in flow collections, but could be an error in block mappings\n\t\t\t\t\t// Only warn in strict mode for non-flow contexts\n\t\t\t\t\tif sv.strict && !unicode.IsSpace(rune(line[colNum+1])) && !strings.Contains(line, \"{\") && !strings.Contains(line, \"[\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:        colNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Colon not followed by space in mapping\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: \":\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFound:         string(line[colNum:min(colNum+2, len(line))]),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix:  \"Add space after colon\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"invalid_spacing\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for unclosed strings at end of line\n\t\tif inString {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\t\tLine:          lineNum + 1,\n\t\t\t\tColumn:        len(line) + 1,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unclosed string at end of line\",\n\t\t\t\tDelimiterType: string(stringChar),\n\t\t\t\tFound:         \"end of line\",\n\t\t\t\tExpected:      string(stringChar),\n\t\t\t\tSuggestedFix:  \"Close the string with matching quote\",\n\t\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unclosed_string\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for unmatched delimiters at end of file\n\tfor _, paren := range parenStack {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\tLine:          len(lines),\n\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unclosed parenthesis at end of file\",\n\t\t\tDelimiterType: string(paren),\n\t\t\tExpected:      \")\",\n\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_paren\",\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, bracket := range bracketStack {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\tLine:          len(lines),\n\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unclosed bracket at end of file\",\n\t\t\tDelimiterType: string(bracket),\n\t\t\tExpected:      \"]\",\n\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_bracket\",\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, brace := range braceStack {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, DelimiterError{\n\t\t\tLine:          len(lines),\n\t\t\tMessage:       \"Unclosed brace at end of file\",\n\t\t\tDelimiterType: string(brace),\n\t\t\tExpected:      \"}\",\n\t\t\tErrorCategory: \"unmatched_brace\",\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// DetectStructureErrors detects structural issues in YAML.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) DetectStructureErrors(yamlContent string) []StructureError {\n\terrors := make([]StructureError, 0)\n\n\t// Parse YAML to detect structure issues\n\tvar node yaml.Node\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Parse errors are already captured in SyntaxErrors\n\t\treturn errors\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for duplicate keys\n\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkDuplicateKeys(&node, yamlContent)...)\n\n\t// Check for invalid mapping structures\n\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkInvalidMappings(&node, yamlContent)...)\n\n\t// Check for invalid sequence structures\n\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkInvalidSequences(&node, yamlContent)...)\n\n\t// Check for mixed content types\n\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkMixedContent(&node, yamlContent)...)\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// checkDuplicateKeys checks for duplicate mapping keys.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) checkDuplicateKeys(node *yaml.Node, content string) []StructureError {\n\tvar errors []StructureError\n\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn errors\n\t}\n\n\t// Check this node if it's a mapping\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.MappingNode {\n\t\tseenKeys := make(map[string]int)\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(node.Content); i += 2 {\n\t\t\tif i+1 < len(node.Content) {\n\t\t\t\tkeyNode := node.Content[i]\n\t\t\t\tif keyNode.Kind == yaml.ScalarNode && keyNode.Value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tkey := keyNode.Value\n\t\t\t\t\tif firstLine, seen := seenKeys[key]; seen {\n\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, StructureError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:     \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:         keyNode.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:      fmt.Sprintf(\"Duplicate key %q detected\", key),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDuplicateKey: key,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLocation:     fmt.Sprintf(\"first occurrence at line %d\", firstLine),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode:    ErrCodeDuplicateKey,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tseenKeys[key] = keyNode.Line\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkDuplicateKeys(child, content)...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// checkInvalidMappings checks for invalid mapping structures.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) checkInvalidMappings(node *yaml.Node, content string) []StructureError {\n\tvar errors []StructureError\n\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn errors\n\t}\n\n\t// Check this node if it's a mapping\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.MappingNode {\n\t\t// Mappings must have an even number of children (key-value pairs)\n\t\tif len(node.Content)%2 != 0 {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, StructureError{\n\t\t\t\tFilePath:  \"\",\n\t\t\t\tLine:      node.Line,\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Mapping has odd number of nodes (missing value for key)\",\n\t\t\t\tLocation:  fmt.Sprintf(\"mapping at line %d\", node.Line),\n\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeInvalidStructure,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for non-scalar keys (keys must be scalar)\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(node.Content); i += 2 {\n\t\t\tif i < len(node.Content) {\n\t\t\t\tkeyNode := node.Content[i]\n\t\t\t\tif keyNode.Kind != yaml.ScalarNode {\n\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, StructureError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:  \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:      keyNode.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Mapping key must be scalar, found %s\", kindToString(keyNode.Kind)),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tLocation:  fmt.Sprintf(\"key at line %d\", keyNode.Line),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeInvalidStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkInvalidMappings(child, content)...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// checkInvalidSequences checks for invalid sequence structures.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) checkInvalidSequences(node *yaml.Node, content string) []StructureError {\n\tvar errors []StructureError\n\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn errors\n\t}\n\n\t// Check this node if it's a sequence\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.SequenceNode {\n\t\t// Check for inconsistent indentation in multi-line sequences\n\t\tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n\t\tfor i, child := range node.Content {\n\t\t\tif child.Line-1 < len(lines) {\n\t\t\t\tline := lines[child.Line-1]\n\t\t\t\t// Check if sequence items have consistent indentation\n\t\t\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, \" \\t\")\n\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"- \") {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Valid sequence item\n\t\t\t\t} else if !strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, \"-\") && trimmed != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Potential structure issue - sequence item without dash\n\t\t\t\t\tif child.Kind == yaml.ScalarNode {\n\t\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, StructureError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:  \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:      child.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Sequence item should start with '-'\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLocation:  fmt.Sprintf(\"item %d at line %d\", i+1, child.Line),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeInvalidStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkInvalidSequences(child, content)...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// checkMixedContent checks for mixed content types in collections.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) checkMixedContent(node *yaml.Node, content string) []StructureError {\n\tvar errors []StructureError\n\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn errors\n\t}\n\n\t// Check sequences for mixed types\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.SequenceNode && len(node.Content) > 1 {\n\t\tfirstKind := node.Content[0].Kind\n\t\tfor i, child := range node.Content {\n\t\t\tif child.Kind != firstKind && child.Kind != yaml.ScalarNode {\n\t\t\t\t// Allow mixing scalars with other types, but flag mixed complex types\n\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, StructureError{\n\t\t\t\t\tFilePath:  \"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:      child.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Sequence contains mixed content types (%s and %s)\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tkindToString(firstKind), kindToString(child.Kind)),\n\t\t\t\t\tLocation:  fmt.Sprintf(\"item %d at line %d\", i+1, child.Line),\n\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeInvalidStructure,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, sv.checkMixedContent(child, content)...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// kindToString converts a yaml.Node Kind to a readable string.\nfunc kindToString(kind yaml.Kind) string {\n\tswitch kind {\n\tcase yaml.DocumentNode:\n\t\treturn \"document\"\n\tcase yaml.MappingNode:\n\t\treturn \"mapping\"\n\tcase yaml.SequenceNode:\n\t\treturn \"sequence\"\n\tcase yaml.ScalarNode:\n\t\treturn \"scalar\"\n\tcase yaml.AliasNode:\n\t\treturn \"alias\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetErrorContext provides contextual information around an error location.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) GetErrorContext(content string, line int, contextLines int) SyntaxErrorContext {\n\tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n\ttotalLines := len(lines)\n\n\tstartLine := max(1, line-contextLines)\n\tendLine := min(totalLines, line+contextLines)\n\n\tcontext := SyntaxErrorContext{\n\t\tErrorLine: line,\n\t\tStartLine: startLine,\n\t\tEndLine:   endLine,\n\t\tLines:     lines[startLine-1 : endLine],\n\t\tHasError:  true,\n\t}\n\n\t// Create pointer for error line if available\n\tif line >= startLine && line <= endLine && line <= totalLines {\n\t\terrorLineIdx := line - startLine\n\t\tif errorLineIdx >= 0 && errorLineIdx < len(context.Lines) {\n\t\t\t// Find first non-whitespace character for pointer\n\t\t\terrorLineContent := context.Lines[errorLineIdx]\n\t\t\tindentSpaces := 0\n\t\t\tfor _, ch := range errorLineContent {\n\t\t\t\tif ch == ' ' || ch == '\\t' {\n\t\t\t\t\tindentSpaces++\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontext.IndentSpaces = indentSpaces\n\t\t\tcontext.Pointer = \"^\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn context\n}\n\n// convertParseError converts a yaml.v3 parse error to SyntaxError.\n// The filePath parameter is optional; if provided, it enables detailed error messages with file path context.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) convertParseError(err error, filePath string) SyntaxError {\n\tse := SyntaxError{\n\t\tErr:      err,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for specific YAML error types using type assertions\n\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n\t\t// This is a YAML type error - provide detailed information\n\t\t// Use FormatYAMLErrorMessage to get structured error details with field paths, expected/actual types, etc.\n\t\tse.Message = FormatYAMLErrorMessage(filePath, typeErr.Errors)\n\t\tse.ErrorCode = ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n\t\treturn se\n\t}\n\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\tse.Message = errMsg\n\n\t// Extract line and column from error message\n\tse.Line = sv.extractErrorLine(errMsg)\n\tse.Column = sv.extractErrorColumn(errMsg)\n\n\t// Set error code based on message content\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"duplicate\") {\n\t\tse.ErrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t} else {\n\t\tse.ErrorCode = ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n\t}\n\n\treturn se\n}\n\n// extractErrorLine extracts line number from error message.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) extractErrorLine(errMsg string) int {\n\t// Try common patterns\n\tpatterns := []string{\n\t\t\"line %d\",\n\t\t\"line %d:\",\n\t\t\"at line %d\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n\t\tvar line int\n\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, pattern, &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn line\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Try regex-like pattern matching\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"line \") {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"line \")\n\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\t\tlineStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n\t\t\tvar line int\n\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(lineStr, \"%d\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn line\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractErrorColumn extracts column number from error message.\nfunc (sv *DefaultSyntaxValidator) extractErrorColumn(errMsg string) int {\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"column \") {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"column \")\n\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\t\tcolStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n\t\t\tvar col int\n\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(colStr, \"%d\", &col); err == nil && col > 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn col\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractQuotedKey extracts a quoted key from a line.\n// Returns the quoted string (including quotes) if found, empty string otherwise.\nfunc extractQuotedKey(line string) string {\n\tif len(line) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for single-quoted key\n\tif line[0] == '\\'' {\n\t\tfor i := 1; i < len(line); i++ {\n\t\t\tif line[i] == '\\'' && (i == 0 || line[i-1] != '\\\\') {\n\t\t\t\treturn line[:i+1]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for double-quoted key\n\tif line[0] == '\"' {\n\t\tfor i := 1; i < len(line); i++ {\n\t\t\tif line[i] == '\"' && (i == 0 || line[i-1] != '\\\\') {\n\t\t\t\treturn line[:i+1]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// Helper functions\n\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\nfunc max(a, b int) int {\n\tif a > b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n","numLines":1156,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1156}}}

}\n49\t//\tfunc (s *MySchema) Validate(value interface{}) error { ... }\n50\t//\n51\t//\tschema := NewMySchema()\n52\t//\terr := schema.Validate(data)\n53\t//\tif err != nil {\n54\t//\t    return fmt.Errorf(\"validation failed: %w\", err)\n55\t//\t}\n56\ttype Schema interface {\n57\t\t// Validate validates the given value against the schema rules.\n58\t\t//\n59\t\t// The value parameter can be of any type:\n60\t\t//   - Primitive types (string, int, float64, bool, etc.)\n61\t\t//   - Struct types for typed validation\n62\t\t//   - map[string]interface{} for dynamic YAML/JSON data\n63\t\t//   - []interface{} for array validation\n64\t\t//   - Any other custom type\n65\t\t//\n66\t\t// Returns nil if the value conforms to the schema rules.\n67\t\t// Returns an error if the value violates any schema constraints.\n68\t\t// The error type may provide additional details about the validation failure.\n69\t\tValidate(value interface{}) error\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\t// SchemaDefinition represents a YAML schema definition for validation.\n73\t//\n74\t// SchemaDefinition defines the expected structure of YAML documents, including field\n75\t// definitions, type constraints, required fields, and validation rules.\n76\t// This implements the Schema interface.\n77\ttype SchemaDefinition struct {\n78\t\t// Type specifies the schema format type.\n79\t\tType SchemaType\n80\t\n81\t\t// Name is an optional identifier for the schema.\n82\t\tName string\n83\t\n84\t\t// Description provides a human-readable description of the schema.\n85\t\tDescription string\n86\t\n87\t\t// Version specifies the schema version for compatibility tracking.\n88\t\tVersion string\n89\t\n90\t\t// RootFields defines the top-level fields in the schema.\n91\t\tRootFields map[string]*FieldDefinition\n92\t\n93\t\t// NestedSchemas contains subschemas for nested structures.\n94\t\tNestedSchemas map[string]Schema\n95\t\n96\t\t// definitions stores reusable schema definitions.\n97\t\tdefinitions map[string]*FieldDefinition\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\t// FieldDefinition defines a single field in the schema.\n101\ttype FieldDefinition struct {\n102\t\t// Type specifies the expected data type.\n103\t\tType string\n104\t\n105\t\t// Description provides a human-readable description.\n106\t\tDescription string\n107\t\n108\t\t// Required specifies whether the field must be present.\n109\t\tRequired bool\n110\t\n111\t\t// Optional specifies whether the field can be omitted.\n112\t\tOptional bool\n113\t\n114\t\t// Min specifies minimum value or length constraint.\n115\t\tMin *int\n116\t\n117\t\t// Max specifies maximum value or length constraint.\n118\t\tMax *int\n119\t\n120\t\t// Pattern specifies a regex pattern for string validation.\n121\t\tPattern string\n122\t\n123\t\t// AllowedValues specifies an enumeration of allowed values.\n124\t\tAllowedValues []interface{}\n125\t\n126\t\t// DefaultValue specifies the default value if missing.\n127\t\tDefaultValue interface{}\n128\t\n129\t\t// NestedSchema defines the schema for nested objects/arrays.\n130\t\tNestedSchema Schema\n131\t\n132\t\t// ArrayItemSchema defines the schema for array items.\n133\t\tArrayItemSchema *FieldDefinition\n134\t\n135\t\t// customValidators stores additional validation functions.\n136\t\tcustomValidators []FieldValidator\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t// SchemaValidator provides schema-based validation for YAML documents.\n140\ttype SchemaValidator struct {\n141\t\tschema  Schema\n142\t\tconfig  *ValidatorConfig\n143\t\tcompiled bool\n144\t}\n145\t\n146\t// NewSchemaValidator creates a new schema validator with the given schema.\n147\t//\n148\t// The validator uses default configuration unless configured otherwise.\n149\t// The schema is compiled on first validation for performance.\n150\tfunc NewSchemaValidator(schema Schema) *SchemaValidator {\n151\t\treturn &SchemaValidator{\n152\t\t\tschema: schema,\n153\t\t\tconfig: DefaultValidatorConfig(),\n154\t\t}\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\t// NewSchemaValidatorWithConfig creates a new schema validator with custom configuration.\n158\t//\n159\t// The configuration controls validation strictness and behavior.\n160\tfunc NewSchemaValidatorWithConfig(schema Schema, config *ValidatorConfig) *SchemaValidator {\n161\t\treturn &SchemaValidator{\n162\t\t\tschema: schema,\n163\t\t\tconfig: config,\n164\t\t}\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// Validate validates YAML data against the schema.\n168\t//\n169\t// Returns a comprehensive SchemaValidationResult with all errors and warnings found.\n170\t// The validation checks:\n171\t// - Required field presence\n172\t// - Field type correctness\n173\t// - Constraint satisfaction (min, max, pattern, allowed values)\n174\t// - Nested structure validation\n175\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) Validate(data interface{}) SchemaValidationResult {\n176\t\tresult := SchemaValidationResult{\n177\t\t\tValid:                true,\n178\t\t\tErrors:               []SchemaValidationError{},\n179\t\t\tWarnings:             []SchemaValidationError{},\n180\t\t\tMissingRequiredFields: []string{},\n181\t\t\tTypeMismatches:       []FieldTypeError{},\n182\t\t\tConstraintViolations: []ConstraintViolation{},\n183\t\t}\n184\t\n185\t\t// Compile schema if not already compiled\n186\t\tif !sv.compiled {\n187\t\t\tif err := sv.compileSchema(); err != nil {\n188\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n189\t\n190\t\t\t\t// Handle YAMLError with structured information\n191\t\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n192\t\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n193\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema compilation failed: %s\", yamlErr.Error()),\n194\t\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: yamlErr.Code(),\n195\t\t\t\t\t})\n196\t\t\t\t} else {\n197\t\t\t\t\t// Handle generic errors\n198\t\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n199\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema compilation failed: %v\", err),\n200\t\t\t\t\t})\n201\t\t\t\t}\n202\t\t\t\treturn result\n203\t\t\t}\n204\t\t\tsv.compiled = true\n205\t\t}\n206\t\n207\t\t// Validate data against schema\n208\t\tif err := sv.schema.Validate(data); err != nil {\n209\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n210\t\n211\t\t\t// Handle YAMLError with structured information\n212\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n213\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n214\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Data validation failed: %v\", yamlErr),\n215\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: yamlErr.Code(),\n216\t\t\t\t})\n217\t\t\t} else {\n218\t\t\t\t// Handle generic errors\n219\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n220\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Data validation failed: %v\", err),\n221\t\t\t\t})\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\t\treturn result\n224\t\t}\n225\t\n226\t\t// For SchemaDefinition, do detailed field validation\n227\t\tif schemaDef, ok := sv.schema.(*SchemaDefinition); ok {\n228\t\t\t// Convert data to map if needed\n229\t\t\tdataMap, ok := data.(map[string]interface{})\n230\t\t\tif !ok {\n231\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n232\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n233\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected map[string]interface{}, got %T\", data),\n234\t\t\t\t})\n235\t\t\t\treturn result\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t\tsv.validateFields(dataMap, schemaDef.RootFields, \"\", &result)\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Set overall valid flag\n241\t\tresult.Valid = !result.HasErrors()\n242\t\n243\t\treturn result\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t// ValidateFile validates a YAML file against the schema.\n247\t//\n248\t// Reads and parses the file, then validates against the schema.\n249\t// Returns SchemaValidationResult with detailed error information.\n250\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) ValidateFile(filePath string) SchemaValidationResult {\n251\t\tresult := SchemaValidationResult{\n252\t\t\tValid:                true,\n253\t\t\tErrors:               []SchemaValidationError{},\n254\t\t\tWarnings:             []SchemaValidationError{},\n255\t\t\tMissingRequiredFields: []string{},\n256\t\t\tTypeMismatches:       []FieldTypeError{},\n257\t\t\tConstraintViolations: []ConstraintViolation{},\n258\t\t}\n259\t\n260\t\t// Read file content\n261\t\tcontent, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)\n262\t\tif err != nil {\n263\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n264\t\n265\t\t\t// FileError type assertion with structured error codes\n266\t\t\tif fileErr, ok := err.(*FileError); ok {\n267\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n268\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %s\", fileErr.Error()),\n269\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: fileErr.Code(),\n270\t\t\t\t})\n271\t\t\t} else if yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n272\t\t\t\t// Handle YAMLError interface\n273\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n274\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %s\", yamlErr.Error()),\n275\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: yamlErr.Code(),\n276\t\t\t\t})\n277\t\t\t} else {\n278\t\t\t\t// Generic error fallback\n279\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n280\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read file: %v\", err),\n281\t\t\t\t})\n282\t\t\t}\n283\t\t\treturn result\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\t// Parse YAML\n287\t\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n288\t\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n289\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n290\t\n291\t\t\t// YAMLError type assertions with structured error codes\n292\t\t\t// Type assertion: *yaml.TypeError captures type mismatch errors from yaml.v3\n293\t\t\t// The Errors field contains a slice of error strings detailing each type mismatch\n294\t\t\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n295\t\t\t\t// Handle yaml.v3 TypeError - provide detailed type error information\n296\t\t\t\t// Use FormatYAMLErrorMessage to get structured error details with field paths, expected/actual types, etc.\n297\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n298\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   FormatYAMLErrorMessage(filePath, typeErr.Errors),\n299\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ErrCodeTypeMismatch,\n300\t\t\t\t})\n301\t\t\t} else if parseErr, ok := err.(*ParseError); ok {\n302\t\t\t\t// Handle ParseError type\n303\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n304\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %s\", parseErr.Error()),\n305\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: parseErr.Code(),\n306\t\t\t\t})\n307\t\t\t} else if syntaxErr, ok := err.(*SyntaxError); ok {\n308\t\t\t\t// Handle SyntaxError type\n309\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n310\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %s\", syntaxErr.Error()),\n311\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: syntaxErr.Code(),\n312\t\t\t\t})\n313\t\t\t} else if typeErr, ok := err.(*TypeMismatchError); ok {\n314\t\t\t\t// Handle TypeMismatchError type\n315\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n316\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %s\", typeErr.Error()),\n317\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: typeErr.Code(),\n318\t\t\t\t})\n319\t\t\t} else if structErr, ok := err.(*StructureError); ok {\n320\t\t\t\t// Handle StructureError type\n321\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n322\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %s\", structErr.Error()),\n323\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: structErr.Code(),\n324\t\t\t\t})\n325\t\t\t} else if yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n326\t\t\t\t// Handle generic YAMLError interface\n327\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n328\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %s\", yamlErr.Error()),\n329\t\t\t\t\tErrorCode: yamlErr.Code(),\n330\t\t\t\t})\n331\t\t\t} else {\n332\t\t\t\t// Generic error fallback\n333\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, SchemaValidationError{\n334\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML: %v\", err),\n335\t\t\t\t})\n336\t\t\t}\n337\t\t\treturn result\n338\t\t}\n339\t\n340\t\t// Validate against schema\n341\t\treturn sv.Validate(data)\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\t// compileSchema compiles and validates the schema definition.\n345\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) compileSchema() error {\n346\t\tif schemaDef, ok := sv.schema.(*SchemaDefinition); ok {\n347\t\t\tif err := schemaDef.Compile(); err != nil {\n348\t\t\t\t// Handle YAMLError with structured information\n349\t\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n350\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"schema compilation failed: %w\", yamlErr)\n351\t\t\t\t}\n352\t\t\t\t// Handle generic errors\n353\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"schema compilation failed: %w\", err)\n354\t\t\t}\n355\t\t}\n356\t\treturn nil\n357\t}\n358\t\n359\t// validateFields validates data fields against their definitions.\n360\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) validateFields(\n361\t\tdata map[string]interface{},\n362\t\tfields map[string]*FieldDefinition,\n363\t\tpathPrefix string,\n364\t\tresult *SchemaValidationResult,\n365\t) {\n366\t\t// Check required fields\n367\t\tfor fieldName, fieldDef := range fields {\n368\t\t\tif fieldDef.Required {\n369\t\t\t\tfullPath := sv.joinPath(pathPrefix, fieldName)\n370\t\t\t\tif _, exists := data[fieldName]; !exists {\n371\t\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n372\t\t\t\t\tresult.MissingRequiredFields = append(result.MissingRequiredFields, fullPath)\n373\t\t\t\t}\n374\t\t\t}\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t// Validate existing fields\n378\t\tfor fieldName, value := range data {\n379\t\t\tfieldDef, exists := fields[fieldName]\n380\t\t\tif !exists {\n381\t\t\t\tif sv.config.StrictMode {\n382\t\t\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, SchemaValidationError{\n383\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:      sv.joinPath(pathPrefix, fieldName),\n384\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:        \"Unknown field in strict mode\",\n385\t\t\t\t\t})\n386\t\t\t\t}\n387\t\t\t\tcontinue\n388\t\t\t}\n389\t\n390\t\t\tfullPath := sv.joinPath(pathPrefix, fieldName)\n391\t\t\tsv.validateField(value, fieldDef, fullPath, result)\n392\t\t}\n393\t}\n394\t\n395\t// validateField validates a single field value against its definition.\n396\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) validateField(\n397\t\tvalue interface{},\n398\t\tfieldDef *FieldDefinition,\n399\t\tfieldPath string,\n400\t\tresult *SchemaValidationResult,\n401\t) {\n402\t\t// Type validation\n403\t\tif !sv.validateType(value, fieldDef.Type) {\n404\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n405\t\t\tresult.TypeMismatches = append(result.TypeMismatches, FieldTypeError{\n406\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n407\t\t\t\tExpectedType: fieldDef.Type,\n408\t\t\t\tActualType:   sv.getTypeName(value),\n409\t\t\t})\n410\t\t\treturn\n411\t\t}\n412\t\n413\t\t// Constraint validation\n414\t\tsv.validateConstraints(value, fieldDef, fieldPath, result)\n415\t\n416\t\t// Nested structure validation\n417\t\tif fieldDef.NestedSchema != nil {\n418\t\t\tif nestedMap, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n419\t\t\t\tnestedPrefix := fieldPath\n420\t\t\t\t// Type assert to SchemaDefinition to access RootFields\n421\t\t\t\tif nestedSchemaDef, ok := fieldDef.NestedSchema.(*SchemaDefinition); ok {\n422\t\t\t\t\tsv.validateFields(nestedMap, nestedSchemaDef.RootFields, nestedPrefix, result)\n423\t\t\t\t}\n424\t\t\t}\n425\t\t}\n426\t\n427\t\t// Array validation\n428\t\tif fieldDef.ArrayItemSchema != nil {\n429\t\t\tif array, ok := value.([]interface{}); ok {\n430\t\t\t\tfor i, item := range array {\n431\t\t\t\t\titemPath := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%d]\", fieldPath, i)\n432\t\t\t\t\tsv.validateField(item, fieldDef.ArrayItemSchema, itemPath, result)\n433\t\t\t\t}\n434\t\t\t}\n435\t\t}\n436\t}\n437\t\n438\t// validateType checks if a value matches the expected type.\n439\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) validateType(value interface{}, expectedType string) bool {\n440\t\tif expectedType == \"\" || expectedType == \"any\" {\n441\t\t\treturn true\n442\t\t}\n443\t\n444\t\tswitch expectedType {\n445\t\tcase \"string\":\n446\t\t\t_, ok := value.(string)\n447\t\t\treturn ok\n448\t\tcase \"int\", \"integer\":\n449\t\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n450\t\t\tcase int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n451\t\t\t\treturn true\n452\t\t\tdefault:\n453\t\t\t\treturn false\n454\t\t\t}\n455\t\tcase \"float\", \"number\":\n456\t\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n457\t\t\tcase float32, float64, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n458\t\t\t\treturn true\n459\t\t\tdefault:\n460\t\t\t\treturn false\n461\t\t\t}\n462\t\tcase \"bool\", \"boolean\":\n463\t\t\t_, ok := value.(bool)\n464\t\t\treturn ok\n465\t\tcase \"array\", \"list\":\n466\t\t\t_, ok := value.([]interface{})\n467\t\t\treturn ok\n468\t\tcase \"object\", \"map\":\n469\t\t\t_, ok := value.(map[string]interface{})\n470\t\t\treturn ok\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn true // Unknown type, accept\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// validateConstraints checks if a value satisfies its constraints.\n477\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) validateConstraints(\n478\t\tvalue interface{},\n479\t\tfieldDef *FieldDefinition,\n480\t\tfieldPath string,\n481\t\tresult *SchemaValidationResult,\n482\t) {\n483\t\t// Min constraint\n484\t\tif fieldDef.Min != nil {\n485\t\t\tif !sv.checkMinConstraint(value, *fieldDef.Min) {\n486\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n487\t\t\t\tresult.ConstraintViolations = append(result.ConstraintViolations, ConstraintViolation{\n488\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:       fieldPath,\n489\t\t\t\t\tMessage:         fmt.Sprintf(\"Value violates minimum constraint: %d\", *fieldDef.Min),\n490\t\t\t\t\tActualValue:     value,\n491\t\t\t\t\tConstraintValue: *fieldDef.Min,\n492\t\t\t\t})\n493\t\t\t}\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\t// Max constraint\n497\t\tif fieldDef.Max != nil {\n498\t\t\tif !sv.checkMaxConstraint(value, *fieldDef.Max) {\n499\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n500\t\t\t\tresult.ConstraintViolations = append(result.ConstraintViolations, ConstraintViolation{\n501\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:       fieldPath,\n502\t\t\t\t\tMessage:         fmt.Sprintf(\"Value violates maximum constraint: %d\", *fieldDef.Max),\n503\t\t\t\t\tActualValue:     value,\n504\t\t\t\t\tConstraintValue: *fieldDef.Max,\n505\t\t\t\t})\n506\t\t\t}\n507\t\t}\n508\t\n509\t\t// Pattern constraint\n510\t\tif fieldDef.Pattern != \"\" {\n511\t\t\tif strVal, ok := value.(string); ok {\n512\t\t\t\tmatched, err := regexp.MatchString(fieldDef.Pattern, strVal)\n513\t\t\t\tif err != nil || !matched {\n514\t\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n515\t\t\t\t\tresult.ConstraintViolations = append(result.ConstraintViolations, ConstraintViolation{\n516\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:       fieldPath,\n517\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:         fmt.Sprintf(\"Value does not match pattern: %s\", fieldDef.Pattern),\n518\t\t\t\t\t\tActualValue:     value,\n519\t\t\t\t\t\tConstraintValue: fieldDef.Pattern,\n520\t\t\t\t\t})\n521\t\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\t}\n523\t\t}\n524\t\n525\t\t// Allowed values constraint\n526\t\tif len(fieldDef.AllowedValues) > 0 {\n527\t\t\tif !sv.isAllowedValue(value, fieldDef.AllowedValues) {\n528\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = false\n529\t\t\t\tresult.ConstraintViolations = append(result.ConstraintViolations, ConstraintViolation{\n530\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:       fieldPath,\n531\t\t\t\t\tMessage:         fmt.Sprintf(\"Value not in allowed list: %v\", fieldDef.AllowedValues),\n532\t\t\t\t\tActualValue:     value,\n533\t\t\t\t\tConstraintValue: fieldDef.AllowedValues,\n534\t\t\t\t})\n535\t\t\t}\n536\t\t}\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// checkMinConstraint checks if a value satisfies the minimum constraint.\n540\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) checkMinConstraint(value interface{}, min int) bool {\n541\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n542\t\tcase int:\n543\t\t\treturn v >= min\n544\t\tcase int8:\n545\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n546\t\tcase int16:\n547\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n548\t\tcase int32:\n549\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n550\t\tcase int64:\n551\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n552\t\tcase uint:\n553\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n554\t\tcase uint8:\n555\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n556\t\tcase uint16:\n557\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n558\t\tcase uint32:\n559\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n560\t\tcase uint64:\n561\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n562\t\tcase float32:\n563\t\t\treturn float64(v) >= float64(min)\n564\t\tcase float64:\n565\t\t\treturn v >= float64(min)\n566\t\tcase string:\n567\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n568\t\tcase []interface{}:\n569\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n570\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n571\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n572\t\tdefault:\n573\t\t\treturn true\n574\t\t}\n575\t}\n576\t\n577\t// checkMaxConstraint checks if a value satisfies the maximum constraint.\n578\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) checkMaxConstraint(value interface{}, max int) bool {\n579\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n580\t\tcase int:\n581\t\t\treturn v <= max\n582\t\tcase int8:\n583\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n584\t\tcase int16:\n585\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n586\t\tcase int32:\n587\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n588\t\tcase int64:\n589\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n590\t\tcase uint:\n591\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n592\t\tcase uint8:\n593\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n594\t\tcase uint16:\n595\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n596\t\tcase uint32:\n597\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n598\t\tcase uint64:\n599\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n600\t\tcase float32:\n601\t\t\treturn float64(v) <= float64(max)\n602\t\tcase float64:\n603\t\t\treturn v <= float64(max)\n604\t\tcase string:\n605\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n606\t\tcase []interface{}:\n607\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n608\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n609\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n610\t\tdefault:\n611\t\t\treturn true\n612\t\t}\n613\t}\n614\t\n615\t// isAllowedValue checks if a value is in the allowed values list.\n616\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) isAllowedValue(value interface{}, allowed []interface{}) bool {\n617\t\tfor _, allowedVal := range allowed {\n618\t\t\tif sv.valuesEqual(value, allowedVal) {\n619\t\t\t\treturn true\n620\t\t\t}\n621\t\t}\n622\t\treturn false\n623\t}\n624\t\n625\t// valuesEqual compares two values for equality.\n626\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) valuesEqual(a, b interface{}) bool {\n627\t\tif a == nil && b == nil {\n628\t\t\treturn true\n629\t\t}\n630\t\tif a == nil || b == nil {\n631\t\t\treturn false\n632\t\t}\n633\t\n634\t\t// Type-specific comparison\n635\t\tswitch va := a.(type) {\n636\t\tcase string:\n637\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(string); ok {\n638\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n639\t\t\t}\n640\t\tcase int:\n641\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(int); ok {\n642\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n643\t\t\t}\n644\t\tcase float64:\n645\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(float64); ok {\n646\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n647\t\t\t}\n648\t\tcase bool:\n649\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(bool); ok {\n650\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n651\t\t\t}\n652\t\t}\n653\t\n654\t\t// Fallback to interface equality\n655\t\treturn a == b\n656\t}\n657\t\n658\t// getTypeName returns the type name of a value.\n659\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) getTypeName(value interface{}) string {\n660\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n661\t\tcase string:\n662\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n663\t\tcase int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n664\t\t\treturn \"integer\"\n665\t\tcase float32, float64:\n666\t\t\treturn \"number\"\n667\t\tcase bool:\n668\t\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n669\t\tcase []interface{}:\n670\t\t\treturn \"array\"\n671\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n672\t\t\treturn \"object\"\n673\t\tdefault:\n674\t\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n675\t\t}\n676\t}\n677\t\n678\t// joinPath joins path components with dot notation.\n679\tfunc (sv *SchemaValidator) joinPath(prefix, component string) string {\n680\t\tif prefix == \"\" {\n681\t\t\treturn component\n682\t\t}\n683\t\treturn prefix + \".\" + component\n684\t}\n685\t\n686\t// LoadSchema loads a schema definition from a file.\n687\t//\n688\t// Supports JSON and YAML schema files. Returns a compiled SchemaDefinition object.\n689\tfunc LoadSchema(schemaPath string) (*SchemaDefinition, error) {\n690\t\t// Read file content\n691\t\tcontent, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)\n692\t\tif err != nil {\n693\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n694\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read schema file: %v\", err),\n695\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n696\t\t\t}\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Detect format and parse\n700\t\text := strings.ToLower(schemaPath[strings.LastIndex(schemaPath, \".\"):])\n701\t\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n702\t\n703\t\tswitch ext {\n704\t\tcase \".json\":\n705\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n706\t\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n707\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse JSON schema: %v\", err),\n708\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n709\t\t\t\t}\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\tcase \".yaml\", \".yml\":\n712\t\t\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n713\t\t\t\t// Type assertion: *yaml.TypeError captures type mismatch errors from yaml.v3\n714\t\t\t\t// The Errors field contains a slice of error strings detailing each type mismatch\n715\t\t\t\t// This preserves error information through the type assertion\n716\t\t\t\tif typeErr, ok := err.(*yaml.TypeError); ok {\n717\t\t\t\t\t// Provide detailed type error information\n718\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n719\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML schema: %v\", typeErr.Errors),\n720\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n721\t\t\t\t\t}\n722\t\t\t\t}\n723\t\t\t\t// Generic error fallback\n724\t\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n725\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse YAML schema: %v\", err),\n726\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n727\t\t\t\t}\n728\t\t\t}\n729\t\tdefault:\n730\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n731\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Unsupported schema format: %s\", ext),\n732\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n733\t\t\t}\n734\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t// Build schema from parsed data\n737\t\tschemaDef, err := buildSchemaFromData(data)\n738\t\tif err != nil {\n739\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n740\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to build schema: %v\", err),\n741\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n742\t\t\t}\n743\t\t}\n744\t\n745\t\t// Compile the schema\n746\t\tif err := schemaDef.Compile(); err != nil {\n747\t\t\t// Handle YAMLError with structured information\n748\t\t\tif yamlErr, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n749\t\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n750\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compile schema: %v\", yamlErr),\n751\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n752\t\t\t\t}\n753\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\t// Handle generic errors\n755\t\t\treturn nil, &SchemaError{\n756\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compile schema: %v\", err),\n757\t\t\t\tFilePath: schemaPath,\n758\t\t\t}\n759\t\t}\n760\t\n761\t\treturn schemaDef, nil\n762\t}\n763\t\n764\t// buildSchemaFromData constructs a SchemaDefinition from parsed data.\n765\tfunc buildSchemaFromData(data map[string]interface{}) (*SchemaDefinition, error) {\n766\t\tschema := &SchemaDefinition{\n767\t\t\tType:          SchemaTypeJSON,\n768\t\t\tRootFields:    make(map[string]*FieldDefinition),\n769\t\t\tNestedSchemas: make(map[string]Schema),\n770\t\t\tdefinitions:   make(map[string]*FieldDefinition),\n771\t\t}\n772\t\n773\t\t// Extract schema metadata\n774\t\tif name, ok := data[\"name\"].(string); ok {\n775\t\t\tschema.Name = name\n776\t\t}\n777\t\tif desc, ok := data[\"description\"].(string); ok {\n778\t\t\tschema.Description = desc\n779\t\t}\n780\t\tif version, ok := data[\"version\"].(string); ok {\n781\t\t\tschema.Version = version\n782\t\t}\n783\t\n784\t\t// Extract field definitions\n785\t\tif properties, ok := data[\"properties\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n786\t\t\tfor fieldName, fieldData := range properties {\n787\t\t\t\tfieldMap, ok := fieldData.(map[string]interface{})\n788\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n789\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n790\t\t\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t\t\tfieldDef, err := buildFieldDefinition(fieldMap)\n793\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n794\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid field definition for %s: %w\", fieldName, err)\n795\t\t\t\t}\n796\t\n797\t\t\t\t// Check if required\n798\t\t\t\tif required, ok := data[\"required\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n799\t\t\t\t\tfor _, reqField := range required {\n800\t\t\t\t\t\tif reqStr, ok := reqField.(string); ok && reqStr == fieldName {\n801\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldDef.Required = true\n802\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n803\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n804\t\t\t\t\t}\n805\t\t\t\t}\n806\t\n807\t\t\t\tschema.RootFields[fieldName] = fieldDef\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\t}\n810\t\n811\t\treturn schema, nil\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t// buildFieldDefinition constructs a FieldDefinition from field data.\n815\tfunc buildFieldDefinition(data map[string]interface{}) (*FieldDefinition, error) {\n816\t\tfieldDef := &FieldDefinition{}\n817\t\n818\t\t// Extract type\n819\t\tif fieldTyp, ok := data[\"type\"].(string); ok {\n820\t\t\tfieldDef.Type = fieldTyp\n821\t\t}\n822\t\n823\t\t// Extract description\n824\t\tif desc, ok := data[\"description\"].(string); ok {\n825\t\t\tfieldDef.Description = desc\n826\t\t}\n827\t\n828\t\t// Extract min/max constraints\n829\t\tif minVal, ok := data[\"minimum\"]; ok {\n830\t\t\tif minInt, ok := minVal.(int); ok {\n831\t\t\t\tfieldDef.Min = &minInt\n832\t\t\t}\n833\t\t}\n834\t\tif maxVal, ok := data[\"maximum\"]; ok {\n835\t\t\tif maxInt, ok := maxVal.(int); ok {\n836\t\t\t\tfieldDef.Max = &maxInt\n837\t\t\t}\n838\t\t}\n839\t\n840\t\t// Extract pattern\n841\t\tif pattern, ok := data[\"pattern\"].(string); ok {\n842\t\t\tfieldDef.Pattern = pattern\n843\t\t}\n844\t\n845\t\t// Extract allowed values (enum)\n846\t\tif enum, ok := data[\"enum\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n847\t\t\tfieldDef.AllowedValues = enum\n848\t\t}\n849\t\n850\t\t// Extract default value\n851\t\tif defaultVal, ok := data[\"default\"]; ok {\n852\t\t\tfieldDef.DefaultValue = defaultVal\n853\t\t}\n854\t\n855\t\treturn fieldDef, nil\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\t// Compile checks if the schema definition itself is valid and compiles it for use.\n859\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) Compile() error {\n860\t\tif s == nil {\n861\t\t\treturn NewSchemaLoadError(\"\", \"schema is nil\", nil, ErrCodeSchemaInvalid)\n862\t\t}\n863\t\n864\t\t// Validate root fields\n865\t\tfor fieldName, fieldDef := range s.RootFields {\n866\t\t\tif fieldDef == nil {\n867\t\t\t\treturn NewValidationError(\"\", fmt.Sprintf(\"field %s has nil definition\", fieldName), fieldName, \"\", ErrCodeSchemaInvalid, 0, 0, ErrorTypeSchemaValidate, \"\")\n868\t\t\t}\n869\t\t\tif err := s.validateFieldDefinition(fieldDef, fieldName); err != nil {\n870\t\t\t\treturn err\n871\t\t\t}\n872\t\t}\n873\t\n874\t\treturn nil\n875\t}\n876\t\n877\t// Validate validates data against the schema definition.\n878\t//\n879\t// This method satisfies the Schema interface. It validates the given value\n880\t// against the schema rules and returns an error if validation fails.\n881\t//\n882\t// The value parameter can be of any type but should typically be\n883\t// map[string]interface{} for YAML/JSON data validation.\n884\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) Validate(value interface{}) error {\n885\t\t// Check for nil value - only error if there are required fields\n886\t\tif value == nil {\n887\t\t\t// If there are no required fields, nil is valid\n888\t\t\thasRequiredFields := false\n889\t\t\tfor _, fieldDef := range s.RootFields {\n890\t\t\t\tif fieldDef.Required {\n891\t\t\t\t\thasRequiredFields = true\n892\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n893\t\t\t\t}\n894\t\t\t}\n895\t\t\tif hasRequiredFields {\n896\t\t\t\treturn NewValidationError(\"\", \"value cannot be nil when schema has required fields\", \"\", \"\", ErrCodeValidationFailed, 0, 0, ErrorTypeValidation, \"\")\n897\t\t\t}\n898\t\t\treturn nil\n899\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\t// Convert to map if needed\n902\t\tdata, ok := value.(map[string]interface{})\n903\t\tif !ok {\n904\t\t\treturn NewTypeMismatchError(\"\", \"\", \"map[string]interface{}\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value), \"\", 0, ErrCodeTypeMismatch)\n905\t\t}\n906\t\n907\t\t// Validate root fields\n908\t\tfor fieldName, fieldDef := range s.RootFields {\n909\t\t\tif fieldDef.Required {\n910\t\t\t\tif _, exists := data[fieldName]; !exists {\n911\t\t\t\t\treturn NewFieldNotFoundError(\"\", fieldName, 0, ErrCodeRequiredField)\n912\t\t\t\t}\n913\t\t\t}\n914\t\n915\t\t\t// Validate existing field\n916\t\t\tif fieldValue, exists := data[fieldName]; exists {\n917\t\t\t\tif err := s.validateField(fieldValue, fieldDef, fieldName); err != nil {\n918\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n919\t\t\t\t}\n920\t\t\t}\n921\t\t}\n922\t\n923\t\treturn nil\n924\t}\n925\t\n926\t// validateField validates a single field value against its definition.\n927\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) validateField(value interface{}, fieldDef *FieldDefinition, fieldPath string) error {\n928\t\t// Type validation\n929\t\tif !s.validateType(value, fieldDef.Type) {\n930\t\t\treturn NewTypeMismatchError(\"\", fieldPath, fieldDef.Type, s.getTypeName(value), fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value), 0, ErrCodeTypeMismatch)\n931\t\t}\n932\t\n933\t\t// Constraint validation\n934\t\tif err := s.validateConstraints(value, fieldDef, fieldPath); err != nil {\n935\t\t\treturn err\n936\t\t}\n937\t\n938\t\treturn nil\n939\t}\n940\t\n941\t// validateType checks if a value matches the expected type.\n942\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) validateType(value interface{}, expectedType string) bool {\n943\t\tif expectedType == \"\" || expectedType == \"any\" {\n944\t\t\treturn true\n945\t\t}\n946\t\n947\t\tswitch expectedType {\n948\t\tcase \"string\":\n949\t\t\t_, ok := value.(string)\n950\t\t\treturn ok\n951\t\tcase \"int\", \"integer\":\n952\t\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n953\t\t\tcase int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n954\t\t\t\treturn true\n955\t\t\tdefault:\n956\t\t\t\treturn false\n957\t\t\t}\n958\t\tcase \"float\", \"number\":\n959\t\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n960\t\t\tcase float32, float64, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n961\t\t\t\treturn true\n962\t\t\tdefault:\n963\t\t\t\treturn false\n964\t\t\t}\n965\t\tcase \"bool\", \"boolean\":\n966\t\t\t_, ok := value.(bool)\n967\t\t\treturn ok\n968\t\tcase \"array\", \"list\":\n969\t\t\t_, ok := value.([]interface{})\n970\t\t\treturn ok\n971\t\tcase \"object\", \"map\":\n972\t\t\t_, ok := value.(map[string]interface{})\n973\t\t\treturn ok\n974\t\tdefault:\n975\t\t\treturn true // Unknown type, accept\n976\t\t}\n977\t}\n978\t\n979\t// validateConstraints checks if a value satisfies its constraints.\n980\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) validateConstraints(value interface{}, fieldDef *FieldDefinition, fieldPath string) error {\n981\t\t// Min constraint\n982\t\tif fieldDef.Min != nil {\n983\t\t\tif !s.checkMinConstraint(value, *fieldDef.Min) {\n984\t\t\t\treturn NewConstraintError(\"\", fieldPath, \"min\", fmt.Sprintf(\"must be >= %d\", *fieldDef.Min), fmt.Sprintf(\"value violates minimum constraint %d\", *fieldDef.Min), fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value), 0, ErrCodeConstraintViolation)\n985\t\t\t}\n986\t\t}\n987\t\n988\t\t// Max constraint\n989\t\tif fieldDef.Max != nil {\n990\t\t\tif !s.checkMaxConstraint(value, *fieldDef.Max) {\n991\t\t\t\treturn NewConstraintError(\"\", fieldPath, \"max\", fmt.Sprintf(\"must be <= %d\", *fieldDef.Max), fmt.Sprintf(\"value violates maximum constraint %d\", *fieldDef.Max), fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value), 0, ErrCodeConstraintViolation)\n992\t\t\t}\n993\t\t}\n994\t\n995\t\t// Pattern constraint\n996\t\tif fieldDef.Pattern != \"\" {\n997\t\t\tif strVal, ok := value.(string); ok {\n998\t\t\t\tmatched, err := regexp.MatchString(fieldDef.Pattern, strVal)\n999\t\t\t\tif err != nil || !matched {\n1000\t\t\t\t\treturn NewConstraintError(\"\", fieldPath, \"pattern\", fieldDef.Pattern, fmt.Sprintf(\"value does not match pattern '%s'\", fieldDef.Pattern), strVal, 0, ErrCodeConstraintViolation)\n1001\t\t\t\t}\n1002\t\t\t}\n1003\t\t}\n1004\t\n1005\t\t// Allowed values constraint\n1006\t\tif len(fieldDef.AllowedValues) > 0 {\n1007\t\t\tif !s.isAllowedValue(value, fieldDef.AllowedValues) {\n1008\t\t\t\treturn NewConstraintError(\"\", fieldPath, \"enum\", fmt.Sprintf(\"must be one of: %v\", fieldDef.AllowedValues), fmt.Sprintf(\"value not in allowed list\"), fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value), 0, ErrCodeInvalidValue)\n1009\t\t\t}\n1010\t\t}\n1011\t\n1012\t\treturn nil\n1013\t}\n1014\t\n1015\t// checkMinConstraint checks if a value satisfies the minimum constraint.\n1016\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) checkMinConstraint(value interface{}, min int) bool {\n1017\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n1018\t\tcase int:\n1019\t\t\treturn v >= min\n1020\t\tcase int8:\n1021\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1022\t\tcase int16:\n1023\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1024\t\tcase int32:\n1025\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1026\t\tcase int64:\n1027\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1028\t\tcase uint:\n1029\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1030\t\tcase uint8:\n1031\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1032\t\tcase uint16:\n1033\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1034\t\tcase uint32:\n1035\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1036\t\tcase uint64:\n1037\t\t\treturn int(v) >= min\n1038\t\tcase float32:\n1039\t\t\treturn float64(v) >= float64(min)\n1040\t\tcase float64:\n1041\t\t\treturn v >= float64(min)\n1042\t\tcase string:\n1043\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n1044\t\tcase []interface{}:\n1045\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n1046\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n1047\t\t\treturn len(v) >= min\n1048\t\tdefault:\n1049\t\t\treturn true\n1050\t\t}\n1051\t}\n1052\t\n1053\t// checkMaxConstraint checks if a value satisfies the maximum constraint.\n1054\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) checkMaxConstraint(value interface{}, max int) bool {\n1055\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n1056\t\tcase int:\n1057\t\t\treturn v <= max\n1058\t\tcase int8:\n1059\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1060\t\tcase int16:\n1061\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1062\t\tcase int32:\n1063\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1064\t\tcase int64:\n1065\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1066\t\tcase uint:\n1067\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1068\t\tcase uint8:\n1069\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1070\t\tcase uint16:\n1071\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1072\t\tcase uint32:\n1073\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1074\t\tcase uint64:\n1075\t\t\treturn int(v) <= max\n1076\t\tcase float32:\n1077\t\t\treturn float64(v) <= float64(max)\n1078\t\tcase float64:\n1079\t\t\treturn v <= float64(max)\n1080\t\tcase string:\n1081\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n1082\t\tcase []interface{}:\n1083\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n1084\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n1085\t\t\treturn len(v) <= max\n1086\t\tdefault:\n1087\t\t\treturn true\n1088\t\t}\n1089\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t// isAllowedValue checks if a value is in the allowed values list.\n1092\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) isAllowedValue(value interface{}, allowed []interface{}) bool {\n1093\t\tfor _, allowedVal := range allowed {\n1094\t\t\tif s.valuesEqual(value, allowedVal) {\n1095\t\t\t\treturn true\n1096\t\t\t}\n1097\t\t}\n1098\t\treturn false\n1099\t}\n1100\t\n1101\t// valuesEqual compares two values for equality.\n1102\tfunc (s *SchemaDefinition) valuesEqual(a, b interface{}) bool {\n1103\t\tif a == nil && b == nil {\n1104\t\t\treturn true\n1105\t\t}\n1106\t\tif a == nil || b == nil {\n1107\t\t\treturn false\n1108\t\t}\n1109\t\n1110\t\t// Type-specific comparison\n1111\t\tswitch va := a.(type) {\n1112\t\tcase string:\n1113\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(string); ok {\n1114\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n1115\t\t\t}\n1116\t\tcase int:\n1117\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(int); ok {\n1118\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n1119\t\t\t}\n1120\t\tcase float64:\n1121\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(float64); ok {\n1122\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n1123\t\t\t}\n1124\t\tcase bool:\n1125\t\t\tif vb, ok := b.(bool); ok {\n1126\t\t\t\treturn va == vb\n1127\t\t\t}\n1128\t\t}\n1129\t\n1130\t\t// Fallback to interface equality\n1131\t\treturn a == b\n1132\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t// getTypeName returns the type
